docs/decision-log.md:11:| 2026-06-01 | V1 storage = one entity CSV + append-only event logs (reject 14-table schema) | Accepted | 14 tables over-models 20 rows; normalization is a roadmap item | `docs/plan-reconciliation.md` §4–5 |
docs/decision-log.md:26:| 2026-06-09 | **Re-scope the pre-Phase-3 gate** — add **T-003** eval-coverage hardening (generator-agnostic draft contract + adversarial guardrail corpus) ahead of the two hygiene items; retain only the **secrets hook + git-state-enforcement** as Phase-3-blocking | **Accepted** (subsumed by the 2026-06-11 DoD ratification — T-003 is on the ratified done-path; plan review job `bvympilb4` ran 2026-06-09, NO-SHIP → plan revised to add-alongside 2026-06-12) | T-002's baseline scores only deterministic fields ([scripts/eval.py:25](../scripts/eval.py:25)); Phase 3 changes only generated draft text ([scripts/pipeline.py:112](../scripts/pipeline.py:112)), which no golden field observes — so the current baseline cannot verify Phase 3's "meets/beats baseline" trigger | `docs/phase3-prep-slice-plan.md` |
docs/decision-log.md:34:| 2026-06-11 | **Free-first runtime stack (owner-directed)** — every product-runtime tool/service defaults to free / free-tier / self-hostable OSS (no paid subscriptions, no metered overage); the **only paid runtime exception is the Gemini API**: best current production model, freshness- and price-checked at use, **$5 hard total budget**, spend tracked; free-tier limits verified against official pricing at selection time; a tool whose free tier can't carry the slice routes back to the owner. Dev tooling (Claude/Codex) unchanged on existing plans | Accepted (owner-directed) | Owner set the cost rule 2026-06-11 ("use free tool or free tier tools; except Gemini API calls use best model, under $5 is the budget"); sharpens the 2026-06-09 doctrine (Gemini <$5 + free-alternative naming) into the default runtime selection rule; supports the cost-aware public posture (RULES §3 cost-control, §6 verification) | `CLAUDE.md` (Operating Doctrine — Free-first runtime stack); roadmap Phases 3–6 free routes (Slack free tier/Mattermost · Resend free tier/SMTP · Supabase free tier/self-hosted Postgres · self-hosted n8n · Langfuse/Phoenix) |
docs/decision-log.md:35:| 2026-06-11 | **Project identity = prototype run on demand, not an operated service (owner-directed)** — ActivationOps AI is a prototype solving a use case, executed episodically (demo / eval / walkthrough runs); **no 24/7 operation**, hosting, uptime, deployment, or ops requirements as goals; production-scale operation stays a documented Enterprise Expansion Path, never a build target without owner re-decision; any approved future integration phase = transient flow demonstration, not standing infrastructure | Accepted (owner-directed) | Owner clarification 2026-06-11 ("This won't be running 24/7. It is a project or prototype solving a use case"); prevents ops-engineering drift; explains why free tiers suffice (episodic load); consistent with RULES §3 (cost control before scale) and the playbook's Enterprise Expansion Path (document, don't build) | `CLAUDE.md` (Operating Doctrine — Project identity); `docs/enterprise-delivery-playbook.md` (Enterprise Expansion Path) |
docs/decision-log.md:36:| 2026-06-11 | **Effort policy (owner-directed)** — every stage runs at **MAX effort** (full reasoning depth + full rigor), and the effort level is **declared per stage** in the Professional Process Applied block; standing policy common across all the owner's projects (recorded here for this repo); depth maximal, ceremony proportional per RULES §15 anti-bloat | **Superseded** (2026-06-12 owner correction — effort AUTO-ADJUSTS by task for both Claude and Codex; see the 2026-06-12 effort row; the per-stage declaration survives) | Owner directive 2026-06-11 ("dont forget to tell to what effort level to be kept. for each stage right now it is in max this is common for all"); aligns with Codex pinned `xhigh` and the Gemini best-model policy | `CLAUDE.md` (Operating Doctrine — Effort & project isolation) |
docs/decision-log.md:39:| 2026-06-11 | **Project goal / Definition of Done RATIFIED (owner GO)** — ActivationOps AI is **done** when a company-agnostic, de-branded public repo holds a prototype **run on demand** (no 24/7 operation) that solves **merchant onboarding & activation for a local-commerce delivery marketplace** (specific use case kept; fictional platform name pending trademark check) end-to-end: deterministic triage · bounded LLM drafting (best current Gemini, **<$5 total**) measured against the **v2 baseline** · human approval gate · idempotent simulated sends · a walkthrough demo showing the **hold / reject / send** paths · public docs honestly separating built vs designed · $0 runtime beyond Gemini · max effort every stage *(effort sub-clause later superseded — auto-adjust-by-task, 2026-06-12 row; the DoD itself stands)*. **= T-003 → Phase 3 → Phase 7 (pulled forward).** **Phases 4–6 = optional roadmap**, re-decided by the owner only after Phase 3 evidence (if ever built: transient demonstrations, not standing infrastructure); **L4 stays the designed ceiling in the narrative, never the completion bar** | Accepted (owner GO 2026-06-11: "Your call. continue" — use-case specificity delegated to Claude and exercised as the local-commerce marketplace framing; recommendation Codex-cross-checked same day, AGREE ×4) | Closes the project-level DoD/audience question open since 2026-06-01; anchors the recorded objective ("enterprise-grade, interview-defensible AI build"); ends the goal oscillation (agentic dropped 06-01 → reinstated 06-09 → reassessed 06-11); consistent with the prototype-identity + free-first doctrine | Task-log 2026-06-11 (goal reassessment); `docs/roadmap.md` (DoD block); `docs/open-questions.md` (resolved) |
docs/decision-log.md:41:| 2026-06-12 | **OQ-1: rename the v1 source CSV to a company-agnostic filename (owner override at the T-003 grill)** — `git mv` + the single `SOURCE_CSV` re-pin (`scripts/config.py:10`); content stays **byte-identical** (sha256 pin is name-independent; frozen tests reference `C.SOURCE_CSV` → pass unmodified). Residual DoorDash strings inside the fixture *content* (11 dummy-nudge mentions) and the v1 guardrail corpus stay frozen + **provenance-labeled**; at **Phase 7** the owner decides publish-labeled vs exclude-from-public-artifact. **Partial reversal** of the draft-2 "file untouched, name untouched" line (reversal basis: owner directive "dont anything related doordash. keep company agnostic"; new evidence: live grep proved the filename literal is the only runtime-*code* reference — `scripts/config.py:10`; docs + the regenerated `out/audit_log.csv` also carry the old name and are swept/re-named in S1, golden metadata + `.gitignore` being hash/policy-based — making the rename proof-safe; the prior revocation conflated name-rename with content-replacement, which stays revoked). Validation after reversal: 35/35 + eval 20/20\|45/45 re-run post-re-pin at S1 | Accepted (owner, 2026-06-12 grill) | Maximum de-brand without destroying the hash-pinned v1 regression proof; Claude's keep+label recommendation overridden by the owner | `PLAN.md`; `PLAN-REVIEW-LOG.md` (Act 1); `docs/phase3-prep-slice-plan.md` draft 3 (S1, OQ-1); `scripts/config.py:10` |
docs/decision-log.md:44:| 2026-06-12 | **Effort policy UPDATED (owner-directed): AUTO-ADJUST Claude and Codex by task — supersedes the 2026-06-11 blanket "MAX every stage" row** — model + effort routed by the task's demands (ship-gating reviews · architecture · AI behavior · security · public claims · data-model changes → max / Codex `xhigh`; trivial/low-risk → proportionally lighter); the per-stage "Effort:" declaration in the Professional Process Applied block stays (now states the routed level + why); anti-bloat (RULES §15) unchanged; common across the owner's projects | Accepted (owner-directed 2026-06-12: "i said auto adjust the claude and codex according to the task i meant"; confirmed same session: auto-adjust **replaces** blanket MAX) | Owner correction of the recorded doctrine — the 2026-06-11 wording over-encoded the intent; effort should match the task, not a fixed ceiling. **Validated against official guidance (RULES §6, checked 2026-06-12, Anthropic effort docs via the claude-api reference, cached 2026-05-26):** effort is "a dimension to test, not a fixed setting"; "use max when correctness matters more than cost" (= the ship-gating band); "low for subagents or simple tasks"; max "can be prone to overthinking" on simple work — task-routed effort is the documented recommended pattern; routing high-stakes to MAX flat-out is the conservative end of that spectrum, chosen deliberately | `CLAUDE.md` (Operating Doctrine — Effort); supersedes the 2026-06-11 effort row |
docs/decision-log.md:50:| 2026-06-19 | **PIVOT — rebuild as a real, industry-ADOPTABLE, deployed AI product (owner full-liberty GO; plan approved)** — supersede the Python-CLI-only delivery shape (and Phase-7-as-docs/walkthrough) with a single-stack **Next.js + TypeScript + Tailwind + React on Vercel (free tier)** product that **ports** the proven deterministic core (Python kept tagged `v1-python-prototype` + as a differential-test oracle), integrates **real bounded Gemini** (schema-constrained · claims-gatekeeper · mode taxonomy · cost ledger <$5 · model preflight), makes the **eval harness the spine** (scores real LLM output + a real reproducible caught failure), runs on **HYBRID data** (real open-source entities + synthetic activation overlay, free, honestly labeled, untrusted-real-data sanitized), and ships a **deployed desktop review console**. Benchmark = **adoption-grade** (a real marketplace could inherit + run it). **Equal weight** to Strategy/Ops/BA judgment AND deep applied-AI; answers the full **why-chain** (each why names the rejected alternative + its cost); **universally legible**; **desktop-only**; job search in the **background**. **Keeps** the merchant-activation use case, deterministic-first→bounded-LLM order, eval-first, free-first (Gemini sole paid <$5), prototype-not-service (deploy = transient demonstration, not 24/7 ops), and the honesty posture. **Refines/supersedes the 2026-06-11 DoD (row 39) at the deliverable level** (CLI+docs → deployed product); T-003 S1/S2/S3/S5 fold into the rebuild's Phases A–B; `docs/phase3-prep-slice-plan.md` + `PLAN.md` are superseded as the active plan. Execution via `/autopilot`; owner-gated stops = commits · dataset-source check-in · platform-name · public posting · irreversible/external. | Accepted (owner full liberty + plan approved 2026-06-19) | This session's research (2026 US AI demand — deployed+real-LLM+evals are table-stakes, evals the breakout skill; competitive gap — "AI for merchants" commoditized, the governed stalled-long-tail-activation vertical is the wedge) + 4-agent positioning validation (which also corrected stale legal anchors: EU AI Act high-risk→Art 50 transparency; FTC *Air AI*→*Moffatt v. Air Canada*; governance-as-headline→domain-led) + advisor cross-checks; the offline Python artifact read as "designed, not built" | `~/.claude/plans/gentle-forging-starlight.md` (canonical goal · DoD · phases · blindspots) |
docs/decision-log.md:58:| 2026-06-22 | **Judge model → cross-family Groq `openai/gpt-oss-120b` (strict structured output), via a provider-agnostic boundary; default judge provider = Groq (free, new runtime provider)** — REFINES row 56 (which pinned the judge to same-family Gemini Flash-Lite). Owner raised Groq ("free only as well") and asked "which is best for quality / structured / enterprise." Chosen because: (a) **cross-family** judge (Groq gpt-oss judges Gemini-Flash drafts) is the gold-standard self-preference mitigation — "maker ≠ judge" at the model layer, strictly stronger than Flash-Lite-judges-Flash; (b) **strict constrained decoding** guarantees schema-valid per-claim JSON (Gemini's observed parse-failure rate is non-zero, 1/6 in the live snapshot); (c) **free + no lock-in** — calibration is $0 and the boundary stays swappable (Gemini Flash-Lite kept as a configurable alt). New runtime provider `@ai-sdk/groq` + a free `GROQ_API_KEY` — surfaced, not silent (RULES §8); Gemini stays the only *paid* runtime model (Groq is free), so the free-first posture is unchanged. | Accepted (Claude recommendation on the owner's "which is best" ask, 2026-06-22; owner raised Groq; reversible via env) | Decision Reversal Rule: original = Flash-Lite (row 56); changed because cross-family + strict-schema + free dominate on the owner's stated quality/structured/enterprise criteria; impact = P3 becomes free-key-gated (not <$5 spend); privacy unchanged (synthetic/de-identified inputs only); validated at build via the AI SDK Groq strict-mode check (P1) + the calibration metrics (P3) | `docs/spec-semantic-judge.md` (§3 R-ARCH-3, §8b); freshness checked 2026-06-22 (Groq strict outputs + rate limits; promptfoo MIT; Gemini pricing) |
docs/decision-log.md:63:| 2026-06-25 | **Tech-stack selection = best-fit, cost-aware (owner-directed)** — refines the 2026-06-11 free-first runtime rule (row 34) + the 2026-06-12 tooling-ladder (row 37) from a **hard gate** into a **default bias**: pick the tool that best serves the project goal (a credible, modern, bounded multi-agent AI capability, company-agnostic); free / free-tier / self-hostable stays the cost-aware default, but a clearly-better **paid / enterprise-grade** tool is allowed where it **materially serves the goal**, with a named justification + live freshness/price check (`RULES.md` §6) + (consequential picks) **owner sign-off + Codex cross-check**. **Unchanged:** Gemini = cost-tracked LLM, **$5 hard total**; honesty/simulated labels; **prototype-not-service**; dev tooling (Claude/Codex) on existing plans. The near-term scope/sequencing cap (P3-calibration + A1 + A2; row 59 / Codex #4 / AM-6) is **NOT** reopened — this widens *which* tools may be chosen at A3+, not *when* | Accepted (owner-directed 2026-06-25, AskUserQuestion: "Best-fit, cost-aware") | Owner: *"dont limit to free tech stack to what i mentioned … according the goal of the project align it"* — the strict free-only tightening under-served the elevated multi-agent goal; realigns this project to the owner's global "free-first **with the enterprise path alongside**" posture; cost-discipline preserved as the default bias (RULES §3 cost-control, §6 verification still apply) | `CLAUDE.md` (Operating Doctrine — Tech-stack selection); supersedes the hard-gate reading of rows 34 + 37; `docs/plan-multi-agent-execution.md` Constraint #1 (patched) |

docs/decision-log.md:72:| 2026-06-29 | **SLICE 2 close-out — load-reduced BLIND subsample + REFRAMED deliverable-B success criterion (implements option 1, offline half).** Harness-only (`evals/agent-loop.live.test.ts`): a **pre-registered, OUTCOME-BLIND 4 tune + 4 test subsample** (one item per failure mode per split, lowest-definition-order, ORIGINAL splits preserved, `maxIterations=3` kept) — selection by mode + definition order ONLY, never by prior outcome (advisor caught a drift toward outcome-aware hand-placing of the known-hard item; rejected). **Success criterion reframed:** the prior runs were INCOMPLETE because PROVIDER-DEGRADED, not because a floor failed — so the HARD live assertion is now **`detection === N`** (a degraded run fails loudly, never enshrined), and **`test ≥ K` is a REPORTED measurement (`k_repin.test_meets_floor`), NOT a hard pass/fail** (at reduced N, K=floor(rate×4) is coarse and one genuine non-convergence can land the floor red on an otherwise-clean run — a complete authoritative result, reported honestly, never recomposed to go green); K asserted non-vacuous only. Added an OFFLINE composition unit test (machine-checks the rule). `npm run verify` GREEN 306+5. | Accepted (advisor-cross-checked 2026-06-29; **to be confirmed at the batched Codex review** that gates the live run) | Consequential eval-methodology change (how the ship-gating K is judged) → recorded + flagged for the cross-model gate per CLAUDE.md; matches the directive's stated criterion ("confirm detection=full-N before reading K"); offline/reversible, gated by verify | `docs/a3-7-live-run-status.md` "SLICE 2 CLOSE-OUT — PRE-REGISTRATION"; `evals/agent-loop.live.test.ts` |

UC-7's value survives only as the composed-conformance component inside the verifier program (`docs/plan-truth-audit-execution.md`).

docs/review-packets/roadmap-lifecycle-applicability-review.md:183:- **Phase 3 — Persistence + provenance at scale (Supabase/Postgres).** Only when one entity CSV + two logs is genuinely outgrown; migrations; the deferred normalized schema as needed.
docs/review-packets/roadmap-lifecycle-applicability-review.md:201:- **It respects recorded decisions.** "Agentic" stays dropped; offline-first holds; integrations stay deferred; the 14-table schema stays a roadmap item — all consistent with the decision log and reconciliation.
docs/WHY.md:23:**Why a bounded LLM, not an autonomous agent.** The model gets schema-constrained, single-shot drafting behind guardrails. *Rejected:* an agentic loop with tools/autonomy. *Cost:* autonomy multiplies the failure surface (runaway loops, tool abuse, unbounded spend) for little value here, and the AI-SDR market shows fully-autonomous outbound collapsing (75–90% 3-month churn). Bounded + evaluated is better value-per-risk; the "no autonomous agent" stance is deliberate maturity.
docs/WHY.md:63:The shape — **deterministic triage + a typed diagnosis layer → bounded, schema-constrained LLM drafting → a claims-gatekeeper that ties every declared claim to source data → an eval harness → a human-approval gate → audit + cost ledger** — is the reusable answer to *any* "use AI to act on people/accounts at scale, safely" problem where a wrong or fabricated claim is costly. Swap the entity and the blocker taxonomy and the same skeleton serves: SaaS trial-to-paid activation (blocker = setup step not done), patient/member onboarding, lender/KYC document chase, B2B renewal/churn outreach, support-ticket deflection. What stays constant is the *governance spine*: the risky decision is deterministic and replayable, the LLM is bounded and evaluated, every declared claim traces to data, and a human owns the irreversible action. That spine — not the merchant specifics — is the transferable asset, and it's exactly what the documented AI-outreach failure frontier (false claims, churn, regulatory liability) demands.
docs/GLOSSARY.md:10:| **Advisory finding (classifier)** | An F1b classifier-derived relabeling *candidate*: built through the same C2 receipts guard (`makeFinding`, `claim.source: "classifier"`) but carried in a separate array that never merges into the base report's findings and never gates `report.ok`; severity `info`; labeled with its classifier's provenance and non-earned status. | A lead the AI flags for a human to check — "this fee might really be something else" — never a verdict, and it can't change the legal checker's answer by itself. |
docs/GLOSSARY.md:11:| **Anti-theater floor (AM-7)** | A deterministic baseline whose measured performance an LLM component must BEAT on held-out gold, in an owner-gated live run, before its label ("classifier", "judge", "agent") is earned; ties or losses → the label DEFERS and the step stays a tool. The F1b keyword-rule baseline is this floor for the fee line-item classifier. | The dumb-but-honest benchmark the AI must visibly out-score on unseen examples before we're allowed to call it good — otherwise we don't. |
docs/GLOSSARY.md:14:| **Basic service fee** | NYC §20-563 (as amended by LL79/2025): the fee for the service that lets a food service establishment receive delivery/pick-up orders on all the platform's sites and be prominently searchable; capped at 5% of the purchase price per order, or on the calendar-month average (§20-563.3(b)). | The "being listed and findable on the app" fee — max 5%. |
docs/GLOSSARY.md:17:| **Client-side schema composition** | The UCP requirement that a validator assemble ("compose") a payload's full schema locally from cross-referencing files (relative `$ref` resolved against each file's absolute `$id`), rather than fetching one monolithic schema. W2's conformance leg registers every pinned UCP schema by `$id` so ajv resolves the whole graph offline. | The official rulebook comes as many small linked files; the checker gathers all the linked pieces itself before it can judge a document. |
docs/GLOSSARY.md:19:| **Conformance vs truth** | Conformance = the feed matches the SPEC (fields, types, schema). Truth = the feed matches REALITY (the SOR). A feed can be 100% spec-valid and still wrong. | Passing the form-check isn't the same as telling the truth on the form. We check both, and the second is the point. |
docs/GLOSSARY.md:20:| **Conformance-foil (demo beat)** | The D1 demo's closing beat: the SAME UCP catalog-response document is run through the conformance leg (it PASSES — spec-valid) and then the truth leg against the SOR (it is caught misstating a price). The beat's line: *passes the official schema check; still lies.* Both verdicts are computed from the real check results, never narrated. | The demo's punchline: a menu answer can follow the official rulebook perfectly and still quote a wrong price — and the checker shows exactly that. |
docs/GLOSSARY.md:25:| **Differential oracle** | A second, independent implementation run over the same inputs whose verdicts must AGREE with ours — a check on our own checker. W2's is the official cargo-only `ucp-schema` Rust validator, run in CI when present and SKIPPED-LOUD (agreement UNMEASURED) when absent — never a runtime dependency. | A second referee from a different toolmaker; if the two ever disagree, we have a bug to find. |
docs/GLOSSARY.md:29:| **Non-statement-checkable rule** | A codified rule the fee-statement schema cannot verify because it needs data an itemized statement does not carry (e.g. fee-change notice dates for g-1-iv, or a service/searchability obligation) — registered with a written reason rather than faked. | A real legal rule our checker honestly can't test from a bill alone, listed and explained instead of pretended-checked. |
docs/GLOSSARY.md:30:| **Enhanced service fee** | NYC §20-563 (LL79/2025): a fee for services beyond the basic service, chargeable only if the platform also offers the basic-service tier; capped at 20% of the purchase price per order, or on the calendar-month average (§20-563.3(d)). | Optional extras (e.g., marketing) — max 20%, and only allowed if a plain basic plan exists too. |
docs/GLOSSARY.md:33:| **Evidence-cited (finding)** | A reported discrepancy that carries machine-checkable pointers to both sources (e.g., row IDs, timestamps, rule/policy versions) supporting the claim. | Every accusation comes with receipts attached. |
docs/GLOSSARY.md:43:| **Monthly-average alternative** | §20-563.3(a)/(b)/(d): the cap may be satisfied on the calendar-month average — Σ(fees in month) ≤ cap% × Σ(purchase prices in month) — instead of per order. The transaction fee (c) has no averaging alternative. | Passing the cap on the month's average instead of every single order — allowed for three of the four fee types, never for the 3% processing fee. |
docs/GLOSSARY.md:44:| **One-page report (S-9)** | The verifier's output as a single self-contained document — evidence-cited, spec-version pinned, simulated-labeled — rendered two ways over the SAME data: a machine-JSON leg (CLI) and a web view (`/report`). "One page" means one cohesive, printable document per report, not a hard one-physical-page cap; every finding's four receipts stay visible (C2 forbids hiding them), and the print stylesheet keeps a finding from splitting across a page break. | The result written up as one readable (and printable) page — plain sentence per catch, receipts beside it — nothing hidden to make it fit. |
docs/GLOSSARY.md:47:| **Pre-registration (calibration)** | Pinning the pass/fail floors, decision rule, and harness semantics in a committed doc BEFORE a live run executes; floors may only ever tighten pre-run, never move after results are seen; a missed floor ⇒ the label DEFERS (no same-split re-run under the 2026-07-04 arming amendment). | Writing the grading rules down before the exam, in ink — so the bar can't move once the score is known. |
docs/GLOSSARY.md:49:| **Print stylesheet** | A CSS `@media print` block that restyles a page for paper/PDF output — here it hides the nav, footer, and surface toggle, forces the honesty labels and severity colors to print (`print-color-adjust: exact`), and marks each finding `break-inside: avoid` so a catch and its receipts never split across a page break. | The extra styling that makes the on-screen report print to a clean page, with the "simulated" label always visible. |
docs/GLOSSARY.md:50:| **Purchase price base** | "Purchase price of each online order" — the statutory base every cap percentage is measured against (§20-563.3(a)–(d)). Its inclusions/exclusions (tax, tip, pre- vs post-discount) are UNVERIFIED as of 2026-07-03 (source-memo U1) — base-derived verdicts are provisional until resolved. | The order amount the percentages apply to — with an honest asterisk: exactly what counts inside it is still an open question. |
docs/GLOSSARY.md:58:| **Transaction fee** | NYC §20-563.3(c): the card-processing-type fee; capped at 3% of the purchase price per order — no monthly averaging, no refund safe harbor; above 3% only as an exact, documented pass-through of the actual processor charge, with proof available to the department and the establishment on request. | The card-processing fee — a hard 3%, unless they can prove every cent above it is a pure pass-through of real cost. |
docs/GLOSSARY.md:60:| **UCP (Universal Commerce Protocol)** | Google-led open agentic-commerce spec (unveiled 2026-01-11 at NRF; Apache-2.0; spec 2026-04-08 live at ucp.dev/GitHub; RFC-2119 normative language). Food vertical co-developed by DoorDash, Square, Toast, Uber Eats — food schemas pending. Catalog capability is a LIVE-QUERY interface (session-scoped), not a static feed. | The Google-side standard AI assistants use to shop and order. Its menu-lookup part asks the store live, rather than reading a saved copy — which matters for what can drift. |
docs/task-log.md:7:- **Part A — web view leg (C4):** `/report` page — `app/report/page.tsx` (server, metadata) → `components/report/ReportView.tsx` (client, surface toggle ACP/UCP) → renders the COMMITTED golden fixture reports (`expected-report.{acp,ucp}.json`, statically imported, bundled). Plain-words line LEADS each finding; the four C2 receipts (claim · reference-row · rule · severity) always visible; header pins specVersion + matchingMode (plain-decoded) + an unmissable SIMULATED banner (C10). Matches the Ledger-Enterprise idiom (rpt-* over the existing `.ds-*` tokens); print stylesheet → clean one-page print, `break-inside: avoid` per finding, honesty labels forced to print. Route prerenders `○ Static`; $0, zero LLM, zero network. Pure transform in `lib/packs/listings/report-view.ts`.
docs/task-log.md:8:- **Part B — corpus packaging (C9):** top-level `fixtures/README.md` index covering BOTH sets (synthetic-restaurant truth corpus + ucp-conformance-ci) — regen (`fixtures:wedge`/`fixtures:ucp`), how-to-run, taxonomy keying (two axes kept separate, honestly), the `ucp-catalog-response.*` shape-honesty caveat VERBATIM, and **License: pending owner decision** (no LICENSE file added; upstream `ucp-schemas/` Apache-2.0 untouched). No frozen fixture bytes mutated — additive docs only.

RED-GREEN ×7 executed (`docs/reviews/w3-verify-evidence.log`); slice record `docs/reviews/w3-slice-record.md`. NO new npm deps. Honest earned-agent ledger after A3-4: **Drafter EARNED · Strategist DEFERRED · Domain Critic DEFERRED · Router pending A3-5.** Took the eval at face value (advisor: "if it ties, defer — don't rescue the label").

Plan-step `agent` stays "tool"; public count stays "3 agents + a candidate".
docs/task-log.md:55:**Honest conclusion (floor-not-ceiling — advisor + Codex):** the structural discriminator (`caution`) is a finite enum a deterministic baseline computes perfectly → the eval is a NECESSARY anti-theater FLOOR (it FAILS a worse-than-baseline Strategist), NOT a label-earning ceiling → the `strategist` label DEFERS to the A3-3 cross-family judge; the "4 agents" claim = "3 + a candidate".

docs/v1-data-dictionary.md:3:Status: **implemented (T-001, 2026-06-02).** This defines the V1 offline thin-slice data contract for ActivationOps AI. `scripts/` implements it, `out/` holds the generated artifacts, and tests T1–T18 pass. Keep this doc in sync with `scripts/` on any schema/guardrail change.
docs/v1-data-dictionary.md:25:| 11 | Risk level uses a `… Risk` suffix | values `Low Risk`, `Medium Risk`, `High Risk` | Normalize to the canonical enum `Low`/`Medium`/`High` by stripping `" Risk"`; carry as authoritative (§3, §6) |
docs/v1-data-dictionary.md:29:V1 storage = **one entity table + two append-only event logs** (not a 14-table schema; not a database). Location `out/` keeps generated artifacts separate from the read-only source.

docs/research/ll79-source-memo.md:8:▸ *Plain: this is the receipts page for the NYC delivery-fee law our fee-audit engine checks against. It records the exact legal text, the exact dates, what we verified vs. couldn't, and where each fact came from — so anyone can re-check us.*
docs/research/ll79-source-memo.md:16:- **Rule shape:** a four-category fee scheme, each with a percentage cap on the **purchase price of each online order**, with a calendar-month averaging alternative on three of the four:
docs/research/ll79-source-memo.md:33:- **(a) delivery fee — 15%:** *"It shall be unlawful for a third-party food delivery service to charge a food service establishment a delivery fee that totals more than 15% of the purchase price of each online order unless the average of the sum of all delivery fees … in a calendar month totals no more than 15% of the sum of the purchase price of all such orders in such month."* Plus a delivery obligation: *"shall provide delivery to any customer located within at least 1 mile … unless exigent circumstances prevent such delivery."*
docs/research/ll79-source-memo.md:34:- **(b) basic service fee — 5%:** *"a basic service fee that totals more than 5% of the purchase price of each online order unless the average … in a calendar month totals no more than 5% …"* (The bracketed old text — *"any fee or fees, other than a delivery fee and a transaction fee, for the use of their service"* — is shown struck, i.e., **deleted** by LL79.)
docs/research/ll79-source-memo.md:35:- **(c) transaction fee — 3% + pass-through exception:** *"a transaction fee that totals more than 3% of the purchase price of each online order, provided however that a third-party food delivery service may charge … more than 3% … if: (i) such transaction fee is charged … in the same amount as the charge imposed upon the third-party food delivery service for such online order, and (ii) such third-party food delivery service can provide proof of such charge … to both the department and the relevant food service establishment upon request."*
docs/research/ll79-source-memo.md:36:- **(d) category lock + enhanced service fee — 20%:** *"It shall be unlawful … to charge … any fee other than a basic service fee, delivery fee or transaction fee, except that a third-party food delivery service that offers a service for which such … service charges a basic service fee pursuant to this section may charge an enhanced service fee. Such an enhanced service fee may total no more than 20% of the purchase price of each online order, unless the average … in a calendar month totals no more than 20% …"*
docs/research/ll79-source-memo.md:73:- **Monthly averaging window** (subd. a, b, d): a **calendar month** — the compliance denominator is *the sum of the purchase price of all such orders in such month*, not per-order, when the averaging alternative is used.
docs/research/ll79-source-memo.md:86:| V4 | Cap values 15% / 5% / 3% / 20% and their base ("purchase price of each online order") | **VERIFIED (primary)** | S1 §3(a)–(d) |
docs/research/ll79-source-memo.md:90:| U1 | Precise definition of **"purchase price of each online order"** (does it include tax? tip? pre- or post-discount subtotal?) | **UNVERIFIED** | Not in extracted §20-563 text; the base term is quoted verbatim but its own definition (if any) was not located. Load-bearing for base-inflation checks — resolve in F1. |
docs/research/ll79-source-memo.md:104:| **S3** | NYC Council **Legistar**, Int 0762-2024 legislation detail (action history: enactment 5/31/2025, LL 2025/079) | https://legistar.council.nyc.gov/LegislationDetail.aspx?ID=6632578&GUID=874ADD05-D520-4896-BA03-79A18349EADD | 2026-07-03 | **PRIMARY** (official legislative record) |
docs/research/ll79-source-memo.md:118:- Does **not** define "purchase price" — the cap **base** is quoted verbatim, but the audit base's inclusions/exclusions (tax/tip/discount) are UNVERIFIED and must be resolved before base-inflation checks are trusted (U1).

fixtures/synthetic-restaurant/ucp-catalog-response.faithful.json:3:  "note": "CONSTRUCTED SIMULATION of a UCP catalog-capability response shape (our interpretation; UCP food schemas pending as of 2026-07-02). Not recorded from any real marketplace.",
docs/PUBLICATION.md:23:**The headline, machine-checked in CI:** a catalog response can pass the official UCP schema validation and still lie about the price. The exhibit file (`fixtures/ucp-conformance-ci/valid/conformant-but-false.json`) passes conformance and fails the truth leg in the same test run. Spec-valid is not true — which is precisely why a conformance checker alone can't hold the referee seat.
docs/PUBLICATION.md:27:**The fee-audit leg:** NYC § 20-563.3's caps, codified from primary legal text into a 17-rule table (11 statement-checkable rules implemented; 6 honestly registered as not checkable from a statement, with written reasons), applied to simulated monthly statements with receipts on every finding: claim, reference row, rule id, severity. An eval asserts no finding exists without all four.
docs/PUBLICATION.md:33:It scored **20/21 accuracy, strictly beating the pinned deterministic baseline (19/21)**, with macro precision 0.971 and zero flip-rate across repetitions. And its label still reads **DEFERRED**, because one per-class recall came in 0.75 against a pre-registered ≥ 0.80 floor.
docs/PUBLICATION.md:39:- **Synthetic corpus, labeled on every surface.** No real merchant data, accounts, or platform access anywhere; no real business-impact claims. The generators are seeded and the committed fixtures are freeze-locked (regenerate ⇒ bytes must match).
docs/PUBLICATION.md:41:- **Real law, real schemas.** The NYC rule table is codified from primary text; the 78 UCP JSON Schemas are pinned verbatim from the official spec repo (Apache-2.0, sha256-locked provenance) at `v2026-04-08`, still the current release as of 2026-07-06.
docs/PUBLICATION.md:42:- **Open questions stay open.** Fee verdicts depending on the statutory "purchase price" base are type-enforced provisional; operator demand is unvalidated; real-world entity matching is harder than the synthetic-controlled matching demonstrated.
docs/PUBLICATION.md:46:737 tests passing (+6 skipped live harnesses) · 8/8 listings drift classes injected and caught (measured) · 33/35 agreement with the official Rust validator with the 2 divergences documented to their root cause (the JSON Schema 2020-12 format-assertion fork) · 17 fee rules codified, 11 implemented + 6 registered non-checkable · classifier 20/21 vs 19/21 baseline, label honestly deferred · $0 of LLM spend on every CLI path, enforced by an import-graph eval.
docs/PUBLICATION.md:52:3. `docs/fee-classifier-calibration-status.md` — the pre-registration, the run, and the DEFER, unedited.
docs/PUBLICATION.md:57:Apache-2.0 (code and synthetic corpus alike; the vendored official UCP schemas keep their upstream Apache-2.0 and provenance record). The repo's first life — ActivationOps AI, a merchant-activation prototype — is archived runnable under `legacy/activation/` with its own labels intact; its verification spine is what this engine grew from.

docs/research/pivot-research-2026-07.md:3:**Date:** 2026-07-02 · **Method:** 2 parallel read-only research threads (research-specialist: industry gap map, ~55 sources checked / ~45 used; opportunity-finder: opportunity screen, ~45 checked / 28 used), Law-11 quarantined, Multi-Source Mandate (≥3 sources / ≥2 platform types on load-bearing claims, dated). Builds on `market-validation-2026-06-22.md` + `merchant-activation-domain-2026-06-19.md` (resumed, not redone). **This digest ranks candidates; it does NOT decide the pivot — that is the owner's call (+ Codex cross-check when a direction is proposed).**
docs/research/pivot-research-2026-07.md:14:- **Agentic ordering is arriving from the platform side:** DoorDash/Uber/Grubhub testing true agentic ordering in Google Gemini since 2026-03 [Retail TouchPoints]; DoorDash photo/prompt AI ordering 2026-06-11 [CNBC]; **Square shipped ChatGPT + Claude ordering 2026-07-01** [Business Wire + VentureBeat + PYMNTS — verified ≥2]; OpenAI ACP product-feed spec live; Google+Square co-developing UCP for local food ordering; note OpenAI wound down in-chat Instant Checkout toward merchant-controlled checkout (spec churn is real) [The Drum + Checkout.com, 2026].
docs/research/pivot-research-2026-07.md:72:FTC ANPRM on delivery fees (2026-04-16, docket FTC-2026-0463; comments closed 2026-05-18) · state AGs urging FTC action (2026-06) · FTC+IL AG v. Grubhub $25M (2024-12; junk fees, driver-pay deception, up to 325K restaurants listed without consent) · NYC tiered fee caps effective 2025-05-31 + open DCWP fee-cap docket + delivery-worker minimum pay $22.13/hr from 2026-04-01 · Seattle App-Based Worker Minimum Payment + Deactivation Rights Ordinance (eff. 2025-01-01) · CA Prop 22 upheld (2024-07) · CA AB 578 (signed 2025-10: cash refunds, not app credits — single-source, UNVERIFIED against bill text). Pattern: the fee-cap era is becoming a **fee-transparency/tier-disclosure era** — the compliance surface is growing even where caps loosened.
docs/research/pivot-research-2026-07.md:84:"2.5–3% of revenue in disputes" (vendor CEO via trade press) · Chowly menu-wipe (single review) · Pindrop/WEF fraud stats (vendor chain) · "60% of refunds are fraud" (podcast title) · AB 578 details (bill text unchecked) · FTC labor-practices track (headline-level) · DoorDash share (56–61%, sources vary) · Loop win-rate/verification-depth internals (marketing claims — a teardown would settle #3's residual gap) · Air Canada Jan-2026 agent incident (do not cite).
docs/research/pivot-research-2026-07.md:90:3. For #1: verify AB 578 bill text; pull NYC Local Law 79 + Admin Code §20-563.3 primary text into a codified rule table; per-merchant economics validation (user-pain-validator or council).
docs/research/pivot-research-2026-07.md:114:- **CORRECTION to this digest's timeline:** UCP was **unveiled 2026-01-11 at NRF** (not "announced 2026-07-01" — that was the Square/local-food angle reaching our sources): open-source spec **live** at ucp.dev + **github.com/Universal-Commerce-Protocol/ucp**, **Apache 2.0** ("Copyright 2026 UCP Authors"), spec version **2026-04-08** published [ucp.dev + developers.googleblog.com + InfoQ 2026-01 — verified ≥2, accessed 2026-07-02].
docs/research/pivot-research-2026-07.md:115:- **Normative style: RFC-2119/8174 MUST/SHOULD** — a real conformance target. Capabilities: `dev.ucp.shopping.{checkout,cart,catalog,order}` + `dev.ucp.common.identity_linking`; extensions declare `requires`; **JSON Schemas per capability** (e.g. `/2026-04-08/schemas/shopping/catalog.json`); "Platforms MUST resolve schemas client-side by fetching and composing base schemas with active extension schemas". Transports: REST + JSON-RPC/**MCP** + A2A + Embedded. Versioning YYYY-MM-DD + `supported_versions` profile mapping.
docs/research/pivot-research-2026-07.md:116:- **FOOD VERTICAL EXISTS — co-developed by DoorDash, Square, Toast, Uber Eats** (ucp.dev lists them as the Food group; "conversational food ordering… nuanced meal customization, real-time availability"), but the 2026-04-08 overview defines **no food-specific schemas yet** — an "emerging vertical". [ucp.dev, accessed 2026-07-02]
docs/research/pivot-research-2026-07.md:128:- **NYC: § 20-563.3 "Fee caps" CONFIRMED as the operative section** (amlegal codelibrary index). **LL 2025/79 effective 2025-06-30** [intro.nyc/local-laws/2025-79] — the digest's "effective 2025-05-31" needs primary re-confirmation (**flagged**; enactment vs effectiveness may explain the gap). Operative language captured (indexed snippet, to re-verify verbatim at codification): *no fee other than a basic service fee, delivery fee, or transaction fee; enhanced service fees chargeable alongside a basic service fee, totaling ≤20% of the purchase price per order, or monthly-average ≤20% alternative.* **Full section text = named module-two build to-do** — amlegal returns HTTP 403 to our fetcher (browser access or NYC.gov PDF needed); LL79 PDF fetched but unparseable in-session (no poppler installed).
docs/research/pivot-research-2026-07.md:129:- **CA AB 578 — UNVERIFIED label RESOLVED (primary-located):** "Food delivery platforms: customer service" (Bauer-Kahan), **signed 2025-10-06** [leginfo.legislature.ca.gov bill_id=202520260AB578]: **full refund** for undelivered/wrong orders (fraud/customer-fault exceptions), gratuity refunded to customer **without deducting from the driver**, partial-order proration of charges/taxes/fees/gratuity, and **human customer-service access** when a chatbot fails. **Assessment: consumer-protection scope — adjacent to module two (statement/fee truth), not core fee-cap material.** The prior "cash refunds not app credits" one-liner was directionally right but incomplete.
docs/research/pivot-research-2026-07.md:139:2. Both Apache-2.0 → an open validator is legally clean and ecosystem-cooperative (intent-frame fit).
docs/research/pivot-research-2026-07.md:143:6. The menu-domain gap (ACP retail-shaped; UCP food emerging) means our restaurant mapping is an honest interpretation layer — label it; watch UCP food-schema publication as a **re-check trigger** (whoever aligns with it first owns the seat).
docs/research/pivot-research-2026-07.md:148:The council's idea-sharpener live-verified and **falsified** implication #1 above: the official UCP org DOES ship a validator — **`ucp-schema` (Rust CLI, v1.4.0 released 2026-06-26, github.com/Universal-Commerce-Protocol/ucp-schema)** — my repo check missed org-level sibling repos. Also live: a third-party **ACP *checkout* validator** (nekuda-ai/acp-validator-cli, 151 tests) and **ucptools.dev** ($9/mo monitoring); an ACP *feed* validator is referenced at agenticcommerce.pro (single-source, 403 on verify — UNVERIFIED). **What remains empty (re-verified through the correction): the truth/drift-vs-POS-system-of-record layer, evidence-grade audit reporting, UCP FOOD schemas (pending publication), and UC-1's money-lines domain.** Consequence for the plan: "first ACP/UCP validator" is DEAD as an identity; the artifact composes/wraps existing validators (Apache-2.0) and lives ABOVE conformance — "spec-valid feeds can still lie." Implications #2–#7 stand; #1 is superseded by this block.

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# Pinned UCP JSON Schemas — `2026-04-08`
     2	
     3	Professional register leads; *plain-English lines are marked ▸*.
     4	
     5	## What this is
     6	
     7	The official **Universal Commerce Protocol (UCP)** JSON Schemas, pinned verbatim
     8	at spec version **`2026-04-08`** (the `v2026-04-08` release tag of the
     9	`Universal-Commerce-Protocol/ucp` spec repository). These are the **conformance
    10	reference** for the listings pack's UCP *conformance* leg — `lib/packs/listings/conformance.ts`
    11	compiles them with `ajv` and checks whether a catalog-response document is
    12	*shaped* the way the protocol requires.
    13	
    14	▸ *Plain: the official rulebook for what a UCP catalog answer must look like. We
    15	downloaded it, froze it at one dated version, and check documents against it.*
    16	
    17	## Provenance (verify before any public claim)
    18	
    19	| Field | Value |
    20	| --- | --- |
    21	| Protocol | Universal Commerce Protocol (UCP) |
    22	| Spec version pinned | `2026-04-08` |
    23	| Source repo | `https://github.com/Universal-Commerce-Protocol/ucp` |
    24	| Source tag | `v2026-04-08` (tag sha `a2d8bf0b8f`) |
    25	| Raw base URL | `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Universal-Commerce-Protocol/ucp/v2026-04-08/source/schemas` |
    26	| Access date | 2026-07-03 |
    27	| Dialect | JSON Schema `draft/2020-12` |
    28	| Files | 78 (`schemas/**`) |
    29	| License | **Apache-2.0** (`LICENSE`; Copyright 2026 UCP Authors) — verified live from the repo `LICENSE` file |
    30	
    31	Per-file `sha256` + `$id` + original repo path are recorded in `PROVENANCE.json`;
    32	a freeze-integrity eval recomputes each hash and asserts it matches, so a pinned
    33	schema cannot be edited without CI catching it.
    34	
    35	## Repo-structure note (divergence recorded)
    36	
    37	The W2 task expected the JSON Schemas to live in the `ucp-schema` repo. Verified
    38	live on 2026-07-03: `ucp-schema` is the **Rust `compose`/`resolve`/`validate`
    39	tool** (the cargo-only differential oracle, `v1.4.0`); the canonical JSON Schemas
    40	live in the **sibling spec repo `ucp`** under `source/schemas/` (served at
    41	`ucp.dev/schemas/...`). These are pinned from that authoritative source at the
    42	spec tag whose name equals our `UCP_PINNED_VERSION`.
    43	
    44	## How composition works (followed, not invented)
    45	
    46	UCP layers an extensibility protocol on top of JSON Schema (per the `ucp-schema`
    47	README, read as reference on 2026-07-03):
    48	
    49	- Schemas cross-reference by **relative `$ref`** resolved against each file's
    50	  absolute `$id` (`https://ucp.dev/schemas/...`). Our loader registers every
    51	  pinned schema by its `$id`, so `ajv` resolves the whole graph client-side —
    52	  this IS the "client-side schema composition" the protocol mandates.
    53	- A catalog *search* response is validated against
    54	  `catalog_search.json#/$defs/search_response` (required: `ucp`, `products`);
    55	  a *lookup* response against `catalog_lookup.json#/$defs/lookup_response`.
    56	- The protocol also carries **vendor vocabulary at schema level** — a `name`
    57	  capability id and `ucp_request` / `ucp_response` / `ucp_shared_request`
    58	  visibility annotations. Our `ajv` runs `strict:false`, so these are treated as
    59	  non-constraining annotations while **every standard validation keyword**
    60	  (`type`/`required`/`pattern`/`minItems`/`minimum`/`format`) is still enforced.
    61	  **Honesty bound:** applying the annotations to derive per-operation views (the
    62	  `resolve` step) is the official Rust tool's job — our runtime does *structural*
    63	  conformance only. The `test:ucp-oracle` CI lane (`scripts-ts/ucp-oracle-diff.mts`)
    64	  is what would catch any divergence, when `cargo` is present.
    65	
    66	▸ *Plain: the rulebook comes in pieces that point at each other; we load all the
    67	pieces so the checker can follow the pointers. The official checker also hides or
    68	shows fields depending on the request type — we don't do that hiding step, so we
    69	check shape, not per-request visibility, and say so.*
