README.md:7:> **Not affiliated with, endorsed by, or connected to** DoorDash, Uber Eats, Grubhub, Square, Toast, OpenAI, Stripe, Google, or any named business or protocol body. This is an independent, company-agnostic prototype. **Everything it runs on is a labeled synthetic corpus** — no real merchant relationship, account, data, or PII anywhere, and no real business-impact claims. All metrics are simulated or measured on synthetic fixtures, and every artifact says so on its face.
README.md:17:1. **Truth leg** — compare a serving copy (an ACP-style feed, or a UCP catalog response) line by line against the merchant's system of record. Deterministic. No LLM anywhere on this path.
README.md:18:2. **Conformance leg** — validate a UCP catalog response against the **78 pinned official UCP JSON Schemas** (spec `v2026-04-08`). A document can be perfectly spec-shaped and still false, which is the point:
README.md:31:node bin/check.mjs check <doc.json> --conformance                # conformance leg (pinned UCP schemas)
README.md:59:- Agentic ordering is live: ChatGPT and Claude can order food via Square (since 2026-07-01), and Google's assistant has placed delivery orders since 2026-03.
README.md:60:- The protocol layer is settling: ACP (OpenAI + Stripe) and UCP (spec `v2026-04-08`, the version pinned here) are both Apache-2.0 and moving fast; UCP's Food vertical schemas were still pending at the pinned tag.
README.md:61:- Fee enforcement is real: NYC's first restaurant-side fee-cap enforcement returned **$875K to 380+ restaurants** (April 2026), and DCWP has an active rulemaking on machine-auditable delivery-fee recordkeeping (hearing July 2026). Trust, not capability, is the #1 barrier named for agentic commerce (Juniper Research, April 2026).
README.md:63:Dated source records for these claims live in `docs/research/` (source lockfile included).
README.md:75:`fixtures/` is a publishable, self-contained test corpus: a synthetic restaurant system-of-record with faithful and deliberately-drifted serving copies, ground-truth manifests, golden reports, 35 UCP conformance CI documents, and simulated monthly fee statements with answer keys. See `fixtures/README.md` for the index, taxonomy keying, and regeneration commands.
README.md:83:Built human-led with AI assistance: Claude Code as planner/builder, OpenAI Codex as adversarial reviewer, with module-boundary review gates and an independent acceptance gate; records live in `docs/reviews/`. These are development tools — **none of them is the product runtime**. This repo never presents itself as AI-free, and never as AI-built without human direction and review.
README.md:87:Apache-2.0 (see `LICENSE`). The vendored official UCP schemas under `fixtures/ucp-schemas/` keep their own upstream Apache-2.0 license and provenance record.
docs/PUBLICATION.md:3:*Publication draft (Pub slice, plan §5). Written 2026-07-06; all dates and claims are as-of that day and source-backed in `docs/research/` (source lockfile included). This document is the postable narrative; the repo README is the technical front door. Human-led, AI-assisted, professionally reviewed.*
docs/PUBLICATION.md:15:1. **Agentic ordering is live.** Since 2026-07-01 you can order food through ChatGPT and Claude via Square; Google's assistant has placed delivery orders since March 2026. An agent doesn't eyeball a menu and shrug at a stale price — it acts on it.
docs/PUBLICATION.md:16:2. **Trust is the named bottleneck.** Juniper Research (April 2026) identifies trust, ahead of every technical concern, as the #1 barrier to agentic-commerce deployment. The protocol layer (ACP from OpenAI+Stripe, UCP with its Food vertical) standardizes *shape*, not *truth*.
docs/PUBLICATION.md:17:3. **Fee enforcement got real.** New York City's first restaurant-side fee-cap enforcement returned $875K to 380+ restaurants (April 2026), the violations were found by hand, and DCWP now has an active rulemaking on machine-auditable delivery-fee recordkeeping (hearing July 2026). The law is asking for exactly the kind of audit this repo automates on synthetic statements.
docs/PUBLICATION.md:23:**The headline, machine-checked in CI:** a catalog response can pass the official UCP schema validation and still lie about the price. The exhibit file (`fixtures/ucp-conformance-ci/valid/conformant-but-false.json`) passes conformance and fails the truth leg in the same test run. Spec-valid is not true — which is precisely why a conformance checker alone can't hold the referee seat.
docs/PUBLICATION.md:41:- **Real law, real schemas.** The NYC rule table is codified from primary text; the 78 UCP JSON Schemas are pinned verbatim from the official spec repo (Apache-2.0, sha256-locked provenance) at `v2026-04-08`, still the current release as of 2026-07-06.
docs/PUBLICATION.md:57:Apache-2.0 (code and synthetic corpus alike; the vendored official UCP schemas keep their upstream Apache-2.0 and provenance record). The repo's first life — ActivationOps AI, a merchant-activation prototype — is archived runnable under `legacy/activation/` with its own labels intact; its verification spine is what this engine grew from.
docs/PUBLICATION.md:59:*Built human-led with AI assistance (Claude Code as planner/builder, OpenAI Codex as adversarial reviewer — development tools, never the product runtime). This work never presents itself as AI-free, and never as AI-built without human direction and review.*
docs/research/merchant-activation-domain-2026-06-19.md:16:**Group A — step-aligned (merchant-side; WHERE known, layer adds WHY):** A1 business/identity verification (legal name/EIN must match IRS or it stalls); A2 menu friction (blank / failed ingestion / QA-blocked); A3 photo rejection (11 documented reasons — resolution, framing, lighting, overlays, people, mismatch…); A4 hours/availability not populated ("tablet off ⇒ store hidden"); A5 banking/payout incomplete (or Stripe fraud flag). — all verified (DoorDash SSIO developer docs + Merchant Help Center).
docs/research/merchant-activation-domain-2026-06-19.md:41:- **"Verification takes weeks"** is a Shopify-community analog, **not** DoorDash evidence (DoorDash/Stripe say "a few days").
docs/research/use-case-backlog.md:17:- **Evidence anchor:** HungryPanda $875K NYC enforcement (2026-04-08, bundling/relabeling documented) · NYC LL79/2025 tiered caps (eff. 2025-05-31) · FTC docket FTC-2026-0463 (Apr 2026) · searched-and-empty for any product (2026-07-02).
docs/research/use-case-backlog.md:23:- **Evidence anchor:** Square ChatGPT/Claude ordering launch (2026-07-01) · DD/UE/GH agentic ordering in Gemini (since 2026-03) · OpenAI ACP feed spec · McKinsey fragmented-commercial-data finding · 42% weekly sync-caused order errors (practitioner-candidate).
docs/research/use-case-backlog.md:24:- **AI shape:** each listed/agent-visible datum = a claim → deterministic verify vs POS/catalog SOR (merchant-permissioned) → drift report, evidence-cited, human-gated corrections. Standards-aligned (ACP/UCP conformance angle).
docs/research/use-case-backlog.md:25:- **Status:** ACTIVE (designed second slice). Verdict: LEAD-POTENTIAL (early). Watch: UCP spec publication; agent-order volume.
docs/research/use-case-backlog.md:50:### UC-7 · ACP/UCP feed conformance tooling (standards play, subset of UC-2)
docs/research/use-case-backlog.md:51:- **Problem:** agent-commerce feed specs (OpenAI ACP; Google+Square UCP for local food ordering, announced 2026-07-01) have no independent conformance/verification tooling — whoever writes it owns the truth-verification seat.
docs/research/use-case-backlog.md:52:- **Status:** HORIZON — activate when UCP publishes; watch AAIF Agentic Commerce WG + W3C Web Payments WG.
docs/research/use-case-backlog.md:66:- UCP spec publication → UC-7/UC-2 activation window.
docs/research/use-case-backlog.md:71:**⚠ UC-7 correction (2026-07-02):** UC-7's "no independent conformance/verification tooling exists" is FALSIFIED as written — official `ucp-schema` (Rust CLI, v1.4.0 2026-06-26) + community validators exist (see pivot-research-2026-07.md → ADDENDUM → ⚠ CORRECTION). The still-open seats: feed/copy-vs-SOR truth · evidence-grade audit reporting · UCP food schemas (pending) · UC-1 money lines. UC-7's value survives only as the composed-conformance component inside the verifier program (`docs/plan-truth-audit-execution.md`).
docs/decision-log.md:30:| 2026-06-09 | **North-star = full human-in-the-loop _agentic_ system (owner-directed)** — reverses the 2026-06-01 "drop agentic framing for V1" decision at the **target level only**: the target architecture becomes a bounded, HITL, eval-gated, observable agentic system; the deterministic core, guardrails, approval gate, and eval harness become the agent's tools + safety rails. V1/foundation stays deterministic + bounded (no autonomy overclaim). | Accepted (owner-directed; Claude recommended the bounded/governed framing) | Owner set the goal as an enterprise-grade, interview-defensible AI build and chose "full agentic"; the defensible version is **governed agency** — the agent *plus* the controls that make it safe — consistent with RULES §3 + the honesty posture | Architecture Blueprint (to be authored); supersedes `docs/plan-reconciliation.md` §3 at target level; ADR to follow |
docs/decision-log.md:31:| 2026-06-09 | **Data architecture = ADD-ALONGSIDE v1/v2 (not replace)** — original CSV + `golden_merchants.v1.json` + `test_t001`/`test_t002` stay FROZEN as the v1 regression proof (CONTENT untouched; **filename-only "untouched" wording SUPERSEDED by the 2026-06-12 OQ-1 rename row below** — the rename via `git mv` keeps content byte-identical, so the hash-pinned proof stands); the de-identified "Curbside Commons" hybrid lane is a NEW `DEMO_CSV` + `golden_merchants.v2.json` + `eval_baseline.v2.json` + `tests/test_t003.py`; "regenerate golden" = create v2, never overwrite v1 | Accepted (Codex 5.5/xhigh NO-SHIP confirmed; matches Claude analysis + owner latitude) | Replacing `SOURCE_CSV` would destroy the hash-pinned T-002 proof asset and violate "source CSV untouched" + "test_t001 additive-only"; add-alongside preserves the proof **and** enables the de-branded public lane | Codex review job `bvympilb4` (NO-SHIP); `docs/phase3-prep-slice-plan.md` (to be revised) |
docs/decision-log.md:34:| 2026-06-11 | **Free-first runtime stack (owner-directed)** — every product-runtime tool/service defaults to free / free-tier / self-hostable OSS (no paid subscriptions, no metered overage); the **only paid runtime exception is the Gemini API**: best current production model, freshness- and price-checked at use, **$5 hard total budget**, spend tracked; free-tier limits verified against official pricing at selection time; a tool whose free tier can't carry the slice routes back to the owner. Dev tooling (Claude/Codex) unchanged on existing plans | Accepted (owner-directed) | Owner set the cost rule 2026-06-11 ("use free tool or free tier tools; except Gemini API calls use best model, under $5 is the budget"); sharpens the 2026-06-09 doctrine (Gemini <$5 + free-alternative naming) into the default runtime selection rule; supports the cost-aware public posture (RULES §3 cost-control, §6 verification) | `CLAUDE.md` (Operating Doctrine — Free-first runtime stack); roadmap Phases 3–6 free routes (Slack free tier/Mattermost · Resend free tier/SMTP · Supabase free tier/self-hosted Postgres · self-hosted n8n · Langfuse/Phoenix) |
docs/decision-log.md:36:| 2026-06-11 | **Effort policy (owner-directed)** — every stage runs at **MAX effort** (full reasoning depth + full rigor), and the effort level is **declared per stage** in the Professional Process Applied block; standing policy common across all the owner's projects (recorded here for this repo); depth maximal, ceremony proportional per RULES §15 anti-bloat | **Superseded** (2026-06-12 owner correction — effort AUTO-ADJUSTS by task for both Claude and Codex; see the 2026-06-12 effort row; the per-stage declaration survives) | Owner directive 2026-06-11 ("dont forget to tell to what effort level to be kept. for each stage right now it is in max this is common for all"); aligns with Codex pinned `xhigh` and the Gemini best-model policy | `CLAUDE.md` (Operating Doctrine — Effort & project isolation) |
docs/decision-log.md:39:| 2026-06-11 | **Project goal / Definition of Done RATIFIED (owner GO)** — ActivationOps AI is **done** when a company-agnostic, de-branded public repo holds a prototype **run on demand** (no 24/7 operation) that solves **merchant onboarding & activation for a local-commerce delivery marketplace** (specific use case kept; fictional platform name pending trademark check) end-to-end: deterministic triage · bounded LLM drafting (best current Gemini, **<$5 total**) measured against the **v2 baseline** · human approval gate · idempotent simulated sends · a walkthrough demo showing the **hold / reject / send** paths · public docs honestly separating built vs designed · $0 runtime beyond Gemini · max effort every stage *(effort sub-clause later superseded — auto-adjust-by-task, 2026-06-12 row; the DoD itself stands)*. **= T-003 → Phase 3 → Phase 7 (pulled forward).** **Phases 4–6 = optional roadmap**, re-decided by the owner only after Phase 3 evidence (if ever built: transient demonstrations, not standing infrastructure); **L4 stays the designed ceiling in the narrative, never the completion bar** | Accepted (owner GO 2026-06-11: "Your call. continue" — use-case specificity delegated to Claude and exercised as the local-commerce marketplace framing; recommendation Codex-cross-checked same day, AGREE ×4) | Closes the project-level DoD/audience question open since 2026-06-01; anchors the recorded objective ("enterprise-grade, interview-defensible AI build"); ends the goal oscillation (agentic dropped 06-01 → reinstated 06-09 → reassessed 06-11); consistent with the prototype-identity + free-first doctrine | Task-log 2026-06-11 (goal reassessment); `docs/roadmap.md` (DoD block); `docs/open-questions.md` (resolved) |
docs/decision-log.md:41:| 2026-06-12 | **OQ-1: rename the v1 source CSV to a company-agnostic filename (owner override at the T-003 grill)** — `git mv` + the single `SOURCE_CSV` re-pin (`scripts/config.py:10`); content stays **byte-identical** (sha256 pin is name-independent; frozen tests reference `C.SOURCE_CSV` → pass unmodified). Residual DoorDash strings inside the fixture *content* (11 dummy-nudge mentions) and the v1 guardrail corpus stay frozen + **provenance-labeled**; at **Phase 7** the owner decides publish-labeled vs exclude-from-public-artifact. **Partial reversal** of the draft-2 "file untouched, name untouched" line (reversal basis: owner directive "dont anything related doordash. keep company agnostic"; new evidence: live grep proved the filename literal is the only runtime-*code* reference — `scripts/config.py:10`; docs + the regenerated `out/audit_log.csv` also carry the old name and are swept/re-named in S1, golden metadata + `.gitignore` being hash/policy-based — making the rename proof-safe; the prior revocation conflated name-rename with content-replacement, which stays revoked). Validation after reversal: 35/35 + eval 20/20\|45/45 re-run post-re-pin at S1 | Accepted (owner, 2026-06-12 grill) | Maximum de-brand without destroying the hash-pinned v1 regression proof; Claude's keep+label recommendation overridden by the owner | `PLAN.md`; `PLAN-REVIEW-LOG.md` (Act 1); `docs/phase3-prep-slice-plan.md` draft 3 (S1, OQ-1); `scripts/config.py:10` |
docs/decision-log.md:44:| 2026-06-12 | **Effort policy UPDATED (owner-directed): AUTO-ADJUST Claude and Codex by task — supersedes the 2026-06-11 blanket "MAX every stage" row** — model + effort routed by the task's demands (ship-gating reviews · architecture · AI behavior · security · public claims · data-model changes → max / Codex `xhigh`; trivial/low-risk → proportionally lighter); the per-stage "Effort:" declaration in the Professional Process Applied block stays (now states the routed level + why); anti-bloat (RULES §15) unchanged; common across the owner's projects | Accepted (owner-directed 2026-06-12: "i said auto adjust the claude and codex according to the task i meant"; confirmed same session: auto-adjust **replaces** blanket MAX) | Owner correction of the recorded doctrine — the 2026-06-11 wording over-encoded the intent; effort should match the task, not a fixed ceiling. **Validated against official guidance (RULES §6, checked 2026-06-12, Anthropic effort docs via the claude-api reference, cached 2026-05-26):** effort is "a dimension to test, not a fixed setting"; "use max when correctness matters more than cost" (= the ship-gating band); "low for subagents or simple tasks"; max "can be prone to overthinking" on simple work — task-routed effort is the documented recommended pattern; routing high-stakes to MAX flat-out is the conservative end of that spectrum, chosen deliberately | `CLAUDE.md` (Operating Doctrine — Effort); supersedes the 2026-06-11 effort row |
docs/decision-log.md:45:| 2026-06-12 | **Context doctrine (owner-directed): minimal · durable · fresh — context auto-adjusts by task, like effort** — (1) load only what the task needs (progressive disclosure; startup-contract docs = fixed base, the rest on demand); (2) durable state lives in the repo, never only in chat (RULES §1); (3) stable-before-volatile ordering for deterministic, cache-friendly rehydration; (4) fresh session + paste-ready resume prompt beats a long degraded context — stage-boundary cuts are the reset policy. Names explicitly what the project already practiced (startup contract, HANDOFF resume prompts, repo-as-source-of-truth, the 2026-06-12 fresh-session rule); common across the owner's projects | Accepted (owner-directed 2026-06-12: "do this proceed next" on Claude's offer to encode) | Pairs with the same-day effort auto-adjust correction — both dials (depth, context) route by task, not fixed settings. Validated against Anthropic context-management guidance (RULES §6, checked 2026-06-12: progressive disclosure, context editing, compaction, file-based memory — via the claude-api reference, cached 2026-05-26) | `CLAUDE.md` (Operating Doctrine — Context); `~/claude-os/docs/CONTINUITY.md`; HANDOFF resume-prompt practice |
docs/decision-log.md:58:| 2026-06-22 | **Judge model → cross-family Groq `openai/gpt-oss-120b` (strict structured output), via a provider-agnostic boundary; default judge provider = Groq (free, new runtime provider)** — REFINES row 56 (which pinned the judge to same-family Gemini Flash-Lite). Owner raised Groq ("free only as well") and asked "which is best for quality / structured / enterprise." Chosen because: (a) **cross-family** judge (Groq gpt-oss judges Gemini-Flash drafts) is the gold-standard self-preference mitigation — "maker ≠ judge" at the model layer, strictly stronger than Flash-Lite-judges-Flash; (b) **strict constrained decoding** guarantees schema-valid per-claim JSON (Gemini's observed parse-failure rate is non-zero, 1/6 in the live snapshot); (c) **free + no lock-in** — calibration is $0 and the boundary stays swappable (Gemini Flash-Lite kept as a configurable alt). New runtime provider `@ai-sdk/groq` + a free `GROQ_API_KEY` — surfaced, not silent (RULES §8); Gemini stays the only *paid* runtime model (Groq is free), so the free-first posture is unchanged. | Accepted (Claude recommendation on the owner's "which is best" ask, 2026-06-22; owner raised Groq; reversible via env) | Decision Reversal Rule: original = Flash-Lite (row 56); changed because cross-family + strict-schema + free dominate on the owner's stated quality/structured/enterprise criteria; impact = P3 becomes free-key-gated (not <$5 spend); privacy unchanged (synthetic/de-identified inputs only); validated at build via the AI SDK Groq strict-mode check (P1) + the calibration metrics (P3) | `docs/spec-semantic-judge.md` (§3 R-ARCH-3, §8b); freshness checked 2026-06-22 (Groq strict outputs + rate limits; promptfoo MIT; Gemini pricing) |
docs/decision-log.md:63:| 2026-06-25 | **Tech-stack selection = best-fit, cost-aware (owner-directed)** — refines the 2026-06-11 free-first runtime rule (row 34) + the 2026-06-12 tooling-ladder (row 37) from a **hard gate** into a **default bias**: pick the tool that best serves the project goal (a credible, modern, bounded multi-agent AI capability, company-agnostic); free / free-tier / self-hostable stays the cost-aware default, but a clearly-better **paid / enterprise-grade** tool is allowed where it **materially serves the goal**, with a named justification + live freshness/price check (`RULES.md` §6) + (consequential picks) **owner sign-off + Codex cross-check**. **Unchanged:** Gemini = cost-tracked LLM, **$5 hard total**; honesty/simulated labels; **prototype-not-service**; dev tooling (Claude/Codex) on existing plans. The near-term scope/sequencing cap (P3-calibration + A1 + A2; row 59 / Codex #4 / AM-6) is **NOT** reopened — this widens *which* tools may be chosen at A3+, not *when* | Accepted (owner-directed 2026-06-25, AskUserQuestion: "Best-fit, cost-aware") | Owner: *"dont limit to free tech stack to what i mentioned … according the goal of the project align it"* — the strict free-only tightening under-served the elevated multi-agent goal; realigns this project to the owner's global "free-first **with the enterprise path alongside**" posture; cost-discipline preserved as the default bias (RULES §3 cost-control, §6 verification still apply) | `CLAUDE.md` (Operating Doctrine — Tech-stack selection); supersedes the hard-gate reading of rows 34 + 37; `docs/plan-multi-agent-execution.md` Constraint #1 (patched) |
docs/decision-log.md:80:| 2026-07-02 | **BUILD ORDER FLIPPED inside the picked composite (owner, via AskUserQuestion): UC-2 FIRST — cross-surface/agent-facing menu-price-availability TRUTH VERIFICATION leads; UC-1 (fee-cap audit) becomes module two on the same engine.** Basis: the owner's clarified intent (demonstrate deep AI proficiency by BENEFITING the DoorDash/UberEats/Grubhub-class industry — platforms as indirect beneficiaries, efficiency/operational/cost) re-weights the ranking: UC-2 is cooperative (platforms are visibly spending on data integrity — DoorDash Preferred Integrations 2026-05; Square live-catalog truth 2026-07-01), sits on the agentic-commerce frontier (DD/UE/GH in Gemini since 2026-03), and the independent-verifier seat is empty by INCENTIVE (no platform audits competitors; syncers won't audit themselves). UC-1 stays fully specified in the backlog + digest (strongest enforcement evidence; adversarial framing is why it no longer leads). Supersedes the same-day "fee-audit wedge" order; the composite itself and all plan-stage gates (council + Codex + owner GO) are unchanged. | Accepted for planning (owner pick 2026-07-02; final ratification at the plan-stage gates) | Intent-frame fit: the lead artifact should benefit the industry it demonstrates to; same spine, no scope growth | `docs/research/use-case-backlog.md`; `docs/research/pivot-research-2026-07.md`; HANDOFF plan-stage prompt |
docs/decision-log.md:82:| 2026-07-02 | **REFRAME ACCEPTED FOR PLANNING (owner, on Claude's independent-judgment proposal): UC-2's lead artifact = an OPEN ACP/UCP conformance + truth-audit toolkit ("the truth layer for agentic commerce") + a self-referential demo (a real AI agent caught ordering from a deliberately-drifted synthetic menu) — instead of a merchant-facing prototype SaaS.** Why: makes "adoption-grade" literally achievable (open tooling the ecosystem can run the week it ships, vs simulated adoption); deletes UC-2's #1 named killer (surface-access/ToS — we validate feeds handed to us + public specs + our own sandboxes, no marketplace scraping); rides a short real timing window (ACP feed spec live; UCP announced 2026-07-01; **no conformance/verification tooling exists** — UC-7 promoted from horizon into UC-2's lead artifact); maximal intent-frame fit (cooperative, platforms/OpenAI/Google/Square all benefit); spec churn flips from risk to moat (validator-author pattern: SSL Labs / npm-audit → the commercial audit layer on top). UC-1 (fee-cap audit) unchanged as module two — same verifier, money-lines domain. **PLUS standing plan-stage directives (owner, same session):** (a) Claude holds the independent-judgment license through research/planning until build — proactively add own suggestions; at build, flip to spec-adherence + escalate; (b) **LEGIBILITY is a hard design constraint on the artifact itself** ("not complex outwards": one-command validator, one-page drift report, a demo needing no explanation — complexity spent inside, simplicity delivered outside); (c) data strategy deliberately spans **free/open + live (ToS-clean only: public specs, own sandbox accounts, merchant-permissioned) + hybrid + synthetic**, with edge-case coverage as an **enumerated taxonomy + measured eval coverage** — never an "all edge cases" overclaim; (d) everything free/free-tier except Gemini (≤$5 hard cap); the demo agent is a demonstration actor — scripted/simulated (labeled) or Gemini-driven within the cap — never Claude/Codex as runtime. | Accepted for planning (owner 2026-07-02 — "your suggestions looks good"; ratification unchanged: council deep-validation + mandatory Codex cross-check + owner GO on the plan) | Claude-originated reframe under the owner's explicit "independent judgment, full liberty" ask; it amends "adoption = metaphorical quality bar" toward partly-literal OSS adoption — surfaced as an owner-level objective change, owner accepted; all honesty/cost/prototype-not-service constraints hold | `docs/research/use-case-backlog.md` (UC-2 + UC-7); `docs/PLAIN-ENGLISH.md`; HANDOFF plan-stage prompt (amended) |
docs/decision-log.md:86:| 2026-07-02 | **Standing directive: DOCUMENTATION STANDARD adopted (floor, not ceiling) — two registers, one truth.** The owner refined the legibility directive: plain must never mean *diluted* — documentation uses the correct technical/business/operational terminology (professional register leads), decoded via first-use explanations + a shared **`docs/GLOSSARY.md`** (created, 21 seed terms); `PLAIN-ENGLISH.md` stays the pure lay entry-point; divergence between registers = a defect. Docs are professionally crafted — visual+text pairing, language flow, tone, nuance, **emotional resonance carried by narrative structure (SCQA), never hype** — built progressively as the project goes. Named frameworks adopted as the floor: **Diátaxis** (doc architecture) · **Minto Pyramid/SCQA** (summaries + narrative) · **C4 model** (architecture visuals: context = lay view) · **ADRs** (existing) · **docs-as-code** (existing) · RULES §7 (Mermaid visual rules, existing). Same-breath maintenance: every meaningful change updates technical docs + PLAIN-ENGLISH + glossary + affected diagrams in the same commit. Full standard: `docs/documentation-standard.md`. | Accepted (owner 2026-07-02 — "floor requirements not ceiling"; frameworks = Claude's suggestion under the standing judgment license, accepted with the directive) | Owner intent: document "how a professional would document, with all fundamentals, methodologies, frameworks"; composes with (does not conflict with) the "not complex outwards" directive — professional ≠ impenetrable, plain ≠ shallow | `docs/documentation-standard.md`; `docs/GLOSSARY.md`; `docs/PLAIN-ENGLISH.md` |
docs/decision-log.md:90:| 2026-07-02 | **PLAN-STAGE GATES RUN: council RESHAPE-PROCEED + Codex CONFIRM-WITH-AMENDMENTS (all 12 accepted) — the reframed direction is now a v1.0-rc plan AWAITING OWNER GO.** Phase 1 UC-2 primary reads completed INLINE (4 subagents died on the shared seat limit — raw error surfaced; not retried) → ADDENDUM + a same-day ⚠ CORRECTION (the "conformance seat empty" claim was falsified in-council: official `ucp-schema` v1.4.0 exists; still-empty = feed-vs-SOR truth · evidence-grade reporting · UCP food schemas · UC-1 money lines). Council (5 sequential agents + synthesis, `shared_reasoning.md`): verdict **RESHAPE-PROCEED, 7 conditions** (S-5 close-out first · UC-1 = primary evidence/AI track · July-16 DCWP owner call · surface-agnostic engine · demo slice-C cut · category claim demoted · split tripwire reviews). Codex cross-check (read-only, xhigh, `codex-guarded`): **CONFIRM-WITH-AMENDMENTS, 12 findings ALL ACCEPTED** — incl. the HARD PRE-BUILD CRUX GATE (G8) blocking UC-2 implementation until the two-part crux resolves (copy-layer persistence + buyer/enforcement authority), S-5 safety wording, confidence downgraded to MEDIUM-conditional, docs-coherence fixes (ledger/backlog annotated), primary-source lockfile. Plan: `docs/plan-truth-audit-execution.md` v1.0-rc; review record `docs/reviews/codex-2026-07-02-pivot-crosscheck.md`. **NOTHING IS DECIDED until the owner GO (O1–O8 in plan §9); no build (incl. S0 commit) before it.** | Gates discharged (council + Codex); Accepted-pending-owner-GO | The two mandatory validation gates on the consequential pivot ran and reconciled primary-model-final; the direction's remaining gate is the owner's | `docs/plan-truth-audit-execution.md`; `shared_reasoning.md` (2026-07-02 evening + synthesis); `docs/reviews/codex-2026-07-02-pivot-crosscheck{,-raw}.md`; `decisions_log.md` |
docs/decision-log.md:92:| 2026-07-02 | **OWNER GO — BUILD NOW (O1 ACCEPTED; the pivot direction is RATIFIED as reshaped).** Owner (verbatim core): "dont have anything related to wait i want to build working prototype now replace look for alternative for the source … do it. It is a personal project so we try to make as much as real possible with real source latest past data or synethicallly created." Rulings derived + recorded: (1) **NO-WAIT DOCTRINE** — every wait-state converts to an inline check or an alternative source: G8 crux gate runs IMMEDIATELY inline (not parked), blocked sources get substitutes (§20-563.3 full text via NYC.gov/LL79-PDF/alternate mirrors instead of the 403'd amlegal; operator voice via reachable trade/forum sources instead of Reddit), UCP-food-schema and enforcement "triggers" are watch-items never blockers. (2) **O4 DCWP July-16 comment = CONSCIOUSLY DECLINED** (personal project; no external/public actions; revivable by owner word). (3) **DATA DOCTRINE** — real-first: real spec texts + real codified law + real latest/past public data wherever ToS-clean-accessible; synthetic fills the rest; every synthetic artifact labeled (RULES §4 unchanged). (4) **COMMITS AUTHORIZED** for this build program (clean slices, explicit paths; push still HELD — no remote). (5) S0's owner-acceptance record for the slice-2 close-out (Codex amendment 2) = THIS row. Standing gates that remain: ≤$5 Gemini cap · deploy · public posting · name adoption (S-11 live checks) · push. Plan `docs/plan-truth-audit-execution.md` flips v1.0-rc → **v1.0 (GO)**. | Accepted (owner GO 2026-07-02) | The plan-stage gates (council + Codex) were discharged same-day; the owner's GO was the final gate and it landed with an explicit no-wait + real-first refinement | `docs/plan-truth-audit-execution.md`; HANDOFF top block; this session's transcript-of-record rows above |
docs/decision-log.md:115:| 2026-07-06 | **PUB SLICE ARMED + EXECUTED (owner: "except design complete all other tasks. resume"). Four gate-deferred decisions taken as RECOMMENDED DEFAULTS (owner AFK at the ask; each reversible or owner-flippable, none irreversible without the owner):** (1) **Naming = descriptive interim, "Commerce Truth Audit"** — NOT a brand adoption, so the S-11 owner gate is untriggered; live screen found "Assay" collided (assay.tools = live 2026 AI-agent devtools product; bare npm `assay` squatted-abandoned 2013) — S-11 stays open for a future brand pick. (2) **License = Apache-2.0 repo-wide** (code + synthetic corpus; canonical text fetched from apache.org; NOTICE added; matches the UCP/ACP Apache-2.0 ecosystem; closes O6). (3) **Publish act = PRIVATE GitHub repo + push, owner flips public** — the irreversible public act stays in the owner's hands; the public-posting hard stop is honored. (4) **Internal process docs published as-is** (RULES §8 satisfied: README fronts the product). News-moment research (live, 2026-07-06): best anchor = the NYC DCWP recordkeeping rulemaking hearing (July 2026, machine-auditable fee-cap records) + Juniper's trust-as-#1-barrier finding; recorded as timing INPUT for the owner, never a blocker (advisor constraint). | Accepted (defaults-taken, owner-ratifiable; surfaced at wrap) | The owner's word armed the slice; the four in-slice decisions followed the surfaced recommendations exactly; anything irreversible (public visibility, brand name) remains owner-held | `docs/PUBLICATION.md`; `docs/reviews/pub-verify-evidence.log`; frontier-advisor consult (PROCEED-WITH-CONSTRAINTS, 2026-07-06) |

This version reads the original PDFs directly from `/Users/sharan_98/Desktop/Claude Code Guide`, re-checks official Tier 1 docs live, and **separates source status explicitly** (directly inspected vs summary-only vs Tier 1 official vs repos inspected vs follow-up gaps).

docs/research/source-intake-review.md:27:- **Key ideas:** "dynamic workflow" = a team of agents spun up by the word `WORKFLOW`. Six-part anatomy: corpus → role/goal → explicit extraction spec → **fan-out (parallel)** → **verify pass (fact-check, report survived-vs-cut)** → one self-contained HTML report with **source on every line**. Three use cases (log-based upgrade guide, X-scraper research report, `~/.claude` setup audit) + a build-your-own template. Requires Claude Code **≥ 2.1.154** with dynamic workflows enabled, paid plan; Prompt 2 needs an Apify token.
docs/research/source-intake-review.md:33:- **Key ideas:** Obsidian = local plain-`.md` vault; Claude Code reads it natively. Commands `/vault-setup`, `/daily`, `/tldr`, `/file-intel` (the last uses **Gemini**). Repos: `earlyaidopters/second-brain` (install scripts) + `kepano/obsidian-skills`. **It explicitly tells you to inject a vault `CLAUDE.md` globally into `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md`.**
docs/research/source-intake-review.md:34:- **Confirms my prior concern directly:** wiring a vault `CLAUDE.md` globally creates a **duplicate, always-loaded source of truth** — against `RULES.md` §1 *and* against official best-practices ("CLAUDE.md is loaded every session, so only include things that apply broadly… Bloated CLAUDE.md files cause Claude to ignore your actual instructions").
docs/research/source-intake-review.md:38:- **Key ideas:** **claudex** wraps the Claude+Codex duo in an autonomous **DRAFT → REVIEW → REVISE → CHECK** loop. Built entirely on **Claude Code's Stop hook** ("the only thing in Claude Code that can force a loop to keep running by BLOCKing Claude's exit") + a YAML state file + CAS transitions. 4 commands: `/claudex:plan` (loop), `/claudex:review` (**read-only in v1**, writes `reviews/`), `/claudex:cancel` (graceful), `/claudex:rollback` (nuclear cleanup; code untouched, log preserved). Install: `github.com/promptadvisers/claudex` (MIT). **Doc is internally inconsistent on the default round count** (text "3"; two diagrams "5").
docs/research/source-intake-review.md:44:## Claude Architect Study Guide — principles cross-checked vs official docs (guide NOT loaded)
docs/research/source-intake-review.md:46:The 55 MB PDF was **not loaded** (rationale above). Its listed principles are confirmed against **official Claude Code docs fetched live 2026-06-02**:
docs/research/source-intake-review.md:51:| Skills for repeatable workflows; don't bloat CLAUDE.md | **CONFIRMED.** best-practices: *"For domain knowledge… use skills instead… without bloating every conversation"*; *"Bloated CLAUDE.md files cause Claude to ignore your actual instructions."* |
docs/research/source-intake-review.md:52:| Subagents for isolated investigation/review; separate review (self-review biased) | **CONFIRMED.** best-practices: *"A fresh context improves code review since Claude won't be biased toward code it just wrote"*; Writer/Reviewer pattern; adversarial review in a fresh subagent. |
docs/research/source-intake-review.md:54:| Give Claude runnable verification checks | **CONFIRMED.** best-practices: *"Give Claude a check it can run: tests, a build, a screenshot"*; *"show evidence rather than asserting success."* |
docs/research/source-intake-review.md:62:## Official Anthropic / Claude Code Tier 1 check (fetched 2026-06-02)

docs/research/pivot-research-2026-07.md:14:- **Agentic ordering is arriving from the platform side:** DoorDash/Uber/Grubhub testing true agentic ordering in Google Gemini since 2026-03 [Retail TouchPoints]; DoorDash photo/prompt AI ordering 2026-06-11 [CNBC]; **Square shipped ChatGPT + Claude ordering 2026-07-01** [Business Wire + VentureBeat + PYMNTS — verified ≥2]; OpenAI ACP product-feed spec live; Google+Square co-developing UCP for local food ordering; note OpenAI wound down in-chat Instant Checkout toward merchant-controlled checkout (spec churn is real) [The Drum + Checkout.com, 2026].
docs/research/pivot-research-2026-07.md:31:- **Evidence it's real NOW:** NYC DCWP's **first-ever restaurant-side fee-cap enforcement** — HungryPanda settlement, **$875K ($580K+ restitution to 380+ restaurants), announced 2026-04-08**; documented tactics = fee **bundling into single line items, frequent relabeling, mischaracterizing overcharges as "promotion deductions"** [nyc.gov press release + Corbett Restaurant Group Apr 2026 — verified ≥2]. NYC's post-settlement structure (effective 2025-05-31, Local Law 79/2025 + Admin Code §20-563.3) is a **tiered scheme** (15% delivery / 5% basic marketplace / optional 20% enhanced / 3% processing; mandatory ≤23% basic-plan alternative) — per-line-item **classification** is now the compliance question [CNBC 2025-06-04 + Bloomberg + Crain's — verified]. SF keeps a permanent 15% core cap. Federal tailwind: **FTC ANPRM "Unfair or Deceptive Fees in Online Food Delivery Services," Federal Register 2026-04-16, docket FTC-2026-0463** (comments closed 2026-05-18), following Grubhub $25M (FTC+IL AG, 2024-12) and Instacart $60M (2025-12); state AGs urged the FTC to regulate delivery pricing (2026-06, Duane Morris alert) [federalregister.gov + ftc.gov — verified ≥2].
docs/research/pivot-research-2026-07.md:40:- **Problem / who has it:** Every ordering surface (DoorDash/UE/GH listings, and now Gemini/ChatGPT/Claude agent surfaces) shows a **copy** of the merchant's menu, not POS truth. A human shrugs at drift; an autonomous agent **transacts on it** — wrong-price orders, unavailable-item cancellations, refunds and error charges that land on the merchant.
docs/research/pivot-research-2026-07.md:41:- **Evidence it's real NOW:** Square's ChatGPT/Claude ordering launch **2026-07-01** makes live-catalog truth its headline safety claim — which solves truth **only inside Square's walled garden** [Business Wire + VentureBeat + PYMNTS — verified ≥2]; DoorDash/Uber/Grubhub agentic ordering tests in Gemini since 2026-03 [Retail TouchPoints]; OpenAI ACP feed spec (refresh ≤ every 15 min) is the agent-visible catalog contract [developers.openai.com/commerce]; McKinsey: agentic tools "struggle with fragmented commercial data… conflicting SKU files, inconsistent pricing history" — merchants must prioritize "clean product feeds, unified inventory, agent-facing content" [McKinsey, Merchants Unleashed]; 42%-weekly-order-error stat says drift persists despite the sync stack (practitioner-candidate); a Chowly menu-wipe complaint (single practitioner review — labeled).
docs/research/pivot-research-2026-07.md:45:- **Viability killers (named):** (1) timing — agent order volume still small mid-2026; leading-edge pain (also the opportunity: the ACP/UCP conformance/verification seat is open NOW and unowned). (2) Surface access — reading marketplace surfaces may hit ToS walls (the unofficial DoorDash MCP servers on GitHub scrape internal APIs and say so); mitigable for merchant-owned surfaces + ACP/UCP feeds, with the marketplace surfaces flagged as the hard 20%. (3) Spec churn (OpenAI's Instant-Checkout wind-down proves it). Model-improvement risk is **neutralized** — stale feeds are a data-integrity problem, not a model-capability problem.
docs/research/pivot-research-2026-07.md:46:- **Adoption-grade prototype shape (sketch):** a merchant-permissioned "truth audit" — POS/catalog snapshot vs ACP-format feed vs simulated surface copies → per-item drift claims, deterministic verification, evidence-cited drift report + human-gated corrections. Standards-aligned (ACP/UCP) so it reads as conformance tooling.
docs/research/pivot-research-2026-07.md:58:Payment networks (Visa/Mastercard agent tokens), PSPs (Stripe shared payment tokens in ACP), and security incumbents (Palo Alto Unit 42, Pindrop) are building the horizontal layer; a solo vertical wedge gets squeezed. Our defensible slice is the **data-truth** side (#2), not identity/fraud. Re-entry trigger: first restaurant-specific agent-fraud incident. (Fraud stats here are vendor/secondary-chain — labeled candidate; the "Air Canada Jan-2026 agent rebooking" anecdote is single-vendor-blog — **do not cite**.)
docs/research/pivot-research-2026-07.md:77:- **UCP spec publication** (Google+Square, local food ordering) — whoever writes the conformance/verification tooling owns the #2 seat. Watch AAIF Agentic Commerce WG + W3C.
docs/research/pivot-research-2026-07.md:79:- **DoorDash moat-erosion thesis** (Citrini note, candidate): commission-free agent ordering (Square 2.9% vs 15–30%) shifts merchant spend from dispute-recovery toward agent-surface integrity — strengthens #2 over #3 over time.
docs/research/pivot-research-2026-07.md:91:4. For #2: ACP/UCP spec primary reads; surface-access legality check (what can be read merchant-permissioned vs ToS-walled).
docs/research/pivot-research-2026-07.md:104:## A. ACP (Agentic Commerce Protocol) — primary read
docs/research/pivot-research-2026-07.md:106:- **Governance/license:** maintained by **OpenAI + Stripe as Founding Maintainers**, "clear path toward broader community governance"; **Apache 2.0**; status **beta**; date-based versioning, releases 2025-09-29 → **2026-04-17 (latest stable; adds Cart, Feed, Orders, Authentication, MCP)** [github.com/agentic-commerce-protocol/agentic-commerce-protocol README, accessed 2026-07-02 — verified vs developers.openai.com/commerce].
docs/research/pivot-research-2026-07.md:108:- **Shape gap (load-bearing, honest):** the feed spec is **retail-shaped — no menu/modifier/allergen/hours model** as of 2026-07-02. Restaurant menus ride the generic item model; our synthetic menu→ACP-feed mapping is itself a contribution and must be labeled as our interpretation, not spec text.
docs/research/pivot-research-2026-07.md:110:- **Conformance tooling: ABSENT** — no validator/test-suite/linter in the official repo or docs (checked 2026-07-02; repo carries OpenAPI specs + JSON Schemas + examples + RFC/SEP process only). Feeds API returns only a generic `400 Bad Request` on invalid payloads. **The conformance seat is empty (ACP), primary-confirmed.**
docs/research/pivot-research-2026-07.md:112:## B. UCP (Universal Commerce Protocol) — status correction + primary read
docs/research/pivot-research-2026-07.md:114:- **CORRECTION to this digest's timeline:** UCP was **unveiled 2026-01-11 at NRF** (not "announced 2026-07-01" — that was the Square/local-food angle reaching our sources): open-source spec **live** at ucp.dev + **github.com/Universal-Commerce-Protocol/ucp**, **Apache 2.0** ("Copyright 2026 UCP Authors"), spec version **2026-04-08** published [ucp.dev + developers.googleblog.com + InfoQ 2026-01 — verified ≥2, accessed 2026-07-02].
docs/research/pivot-research-2026-07.md:116:- **FOOD VERTICAL EXISTS — co-developed by DoorDash, Square, Toast, Uber Eats** (ucp.dev lists them as the Food group; "conversational food ordering… nuanced meal customization, real-time availability"), but the 2026-04-08 overview defines **no food-specific schemas yet** — an "emerging vertical". [ucp.dev, accessed 2026-07-02]
docs/research/pivot-research-2026-07.md:117:- **Conformance tooling: no formal suite** — Playground + reference implementations + code samples only. **The conformance seat is empty (UCP), primary-confirmed.**
docs/research/pivot-research-2026-07.md:121:- **GREEN (confirmed):** both spec corpora **Apache 2.0** → third-party conformance tooling explicitly permitted. **Square Catalog API `ITEMS_READ` OAuth scope** = the designed merchant-permissioned third-party read path (seller reviews + approves scopes) [developer.squareup.com OAuth permissions reference, accessed 2026-07-02]. Own synthetic sandboxes: green by construction.
docs/research/pivot-research-2026-07.md:139:2. Both Apache-2.0 → an open validator is legally clean and ecosystem-cooperative (intent-frame fit).
docs/research/pivot-research-2026-07.md:140:3. **ACP validation = deterministic field/constraint/cross-field checks** (typed limits, enums, conditionals, invariants) — exactly our spine's deterministic-check shape; plus Stable-vs-Draft and date-version awareness.
docs/research/pivot-research-2026-07.md:141:4. **UCP validation = RFC-2119 + JSON-Schema composition conformance** (base + extensions + `requires` + version mapping) — machine-checkable MUST/SHOULD surface.
docs/research/pivot-research-2026-07.md:143:6. The menu-domain gap (ACP retail-shaped; UCP food emerging) means our restaurant mapping is an honest interpretation layer — label it; watch UCP food-schema publication as a **re-check trigger** (whoever aligns with it first owns the seat).
docs/research/pivot-research-2026-07.md:144:7. Spec churn is real (5 ACP releases in 8 months) → version-pinned rule tables + a freshness check in the validator itself (our RULES §6 discipline productized).
docs/research/pivot-research-2026-07.md:148:The council's idea-sharpener live-verified and **falsified** implication #1 above: the official UCP org DOES ship a validator — **`ucp-schema` (Rust CLI, v1.4.0 released 2026-06-26, github.com/Universal-Commerce-Protocol/ucp-schema)** — my repo check missed org-level sibling repos. Also live: a third-party **ACP *checkout* validator** (nekuda-ai/acp-validator-cli, 151 tests) and **ucptools.dev** ($9/mo monitoring); an ACP *feed* validator is referenced at agenticcommerce.pro (single-source, 403 on verify — UNVERIFIED). **What remains empty (re-verified through the correction): the truth/drift-vs-POS-system-of-record layer, evidence-grade audit reporting, UCP FOOD schemas (pending publication), and UC-1's money-lines domain.** Consequence for the plan: "first ACP/UCP validator" is DEAD as an identity; the artifact composes/wraps existing validators (Apache-2.0) and lives ABOVE conformance — "spec-valid feeds can still lie." Implications #2–#7 stand; #1 is superseded by this block.

     1	# F1b Fee-Line Classifier — Live Calibration Status
     2	
     3	**Status (2026-07-05, pre-run): OWNER-ARMED — the pre-registration below is pinned
     4	BEFORE any live call.** The owner armed the run 2026-07-05 ("all four", decision-log),
     5	after the M2 ceremony accepted the F1 module (gate record
     6	`docs/reviews/gate-2026-07-04-m2-f1-module.md`). Results are appended below the
     7	pre-registration after the run; nothing above the RESULTS marker may change after it.
     8	
     9	▸ *Plain: this page locks the grading rules before the AI takes the test, so the
    10	grade can't be argued with afterwards. The owner said GO; the test happens once;
    11	the score gets written here exactly as it lands.*
    12	
    13	This is the fee-classifier analogue of `docs/domain-calibration-status.md`
    14	(R-DHON-3 / R-DCAL-7 precedent). The design + floors live in
    15	`docs/plan-f1b-classifier.md`; this doc pins the RUN protocol and records the outcome.
    16	
    17	## Pre-registration (pinned 2026-07-05, before the run)
    18	
    19	**The floors are `docs/plan-f1b-classifier.md` §3.1 VERBATIM** (including the M2
    20	pre-run amendment: held-out accuracy **≥ 20/21, strictly beating the pinned 19/21
    21	deterministic baseline; a tie = DEFER**; macro precision ≥ 0.85; per-class recall
    22	≥ 0.70 all five labels and ≥ 0.80 on `enhanced_service_fee` +
    23	`not-a-permitted-fee`; flip-rate ≤ 0.15 at K=3 temp 0; macro one-vs-rest Cohen's
    24	κ ≥ 0.60). None of them moves, in either direction.
    25	
    26	**Protocol amendments + mechanics, all pinned pre-run** (frontier-advisor consult
    27	2026-07-05 — PROCEED-WITH-CONSTRAINTS, all adopted):
    28	
    29	1. **DECISION-RULE TIGHTENING (dated amendment, owner-sourced).** Plan §3.2's ELSE
    30	   branch ("tune the prompt on the tune split and re-run") is SUPERSEDED for this
    31	   arming by the owner's stricter 2026-07-04 arming directive (HANDOFF): **any
    32	   missed floor → the label honestly DEFERS; no same-split re-run toward green; no
    33	   post-hoc floor amendment.** One scored pass decides. This tightens (never
    34	   loosens) the registered rule, and is recorded here BEFORE the run.
    35	2. **Prediction of record = rep-0** of K=3 (the `judge-calibration.live.test.ts`
    36	   precedent). Flip-rate = fraction of items whose 3 reps are not unanimous
    37	   (`multiClassFlipRate`, typed multi-class analogue of the ported `flipRate`;
    38	   unit-tested).
    39	3. **Macro-precision 0/0 convention:** the ported `ratio()` returns 0 on 0/0, so a
    40	   never-predicted class contributes precision 0 — macro precision degrades toward
    41	   FAILURE, never toward a pass. Recorded so it cannot be relitigated post-run.
    42	4. **Harness red semantics (the slice-2 precedent):** vitest HARD-asserts RUN
    43	   INTEGRITY only — every call a real `LIVE_CLASSIFIER` verdict (any
    44	   `FAILED_TO_FALLBACK` fails the run loudly = provider-degraded → diagnostic,
    45	   never enshrined, bail rule), K=3 complete, per-class held-out denominators ≥ 3
    46	   (non-vacuous rule; composition-locked upstream). The FLOORS are computed, frozen
    47	   into the snapshot, and judged VERBATIM here for the label decision — they are
    48	   not vitest assertions, so an honest below-floor run records itself rather than
    49	   masquerading as a code regression.
    50	5. **Tune-split use:** Phase A = the full 21-item TUNE split at K=1, prompt-shape
    51	   sanity ONLY (plan §3.2 licenses tune-split use); reported as context; moves no
    52	   floor, decides nothing. One TPM-window cool-down separates it from the scored
    53	   pass; `groq-preflight` runs immediately before the harness starts.
    54	6. **Prompt provenance (leak honesty):** the live prompt's rubric is authored from
    55	   the codified rule table (`docs/research/uc1-rule-table.md`, §20-563.3(d)) and
    56	   the pre-existing `SEVEN_CLASS_TRUE_CATEGORY_NOTE` mapping ONLY — no
    57	   gold-item-specific wording or pattern. The prompt input is the leak-free
    58	   `ClassifierInput` alone; an offline eval walks the ENTIRE gold set asserting no
    59	   prompt carries the answer-key field, the gold rationale, or the §7 stratum name
    60	   (`evals/agents/fee-classifier-live-lane.test.ts`). Residual caveat, stated
    61	   plainly: the prompt author has read the held-out split (unavoidable in this
    62	   repo); the mitigations are this provenance rule, the tune-only adjustment
    63	   discipline, and the pre-registered floors.
    64	7. **Run parameters:** Groq free tier (`openai/gpt-oss-120b`, live-confirmed
    65	   HTTP 200 by preflight 2026-07-05 — the RULES §6 model-id check), $0 — the lane
    66	   has NO paid branch (`lib/agents/fee-classifier.ts` is Groq-only by
    67	   construction); K=3, temp 0, `reasoningEffort:"low"`, `maxOutputTokens` 1,024,
    68	   strict structured outputs; 14s pacing (~4.3 calls/min × ~1,750 reserved ≈ 7,500
    69	   of the ~8,000 TPM window); ONE paced pass, 84 calls total (21 tune + 63
    70	   scored), ≈ 50K tokens ≪ the ~200K TPD window on a fresh calendar day (no TPD
    71	   header exists — freshness argued from date + zero prior runs today, the honest
    72	   A3-7-lesson statement).
    73	8. **Wired ≠ calibrated.** `LIVE_CLASSIFIER_DESIGN.wired` flipped to `true` with
    74	   the lane's code landing (2026-07-05, pre-run — code reality); the "calibrated"
    75	   label is decided ONLY by this run's outcome. A DEFER outcome ends as: wired,
    76	   env-gated, NOT calibrated — a complete, honest, shippable state (plan §3.5).
    77	
    78	**Decision rule (restated):** floors ALL clear → eval-lock the frozen snapshot
    79	(`lib/data/fee-classifier-calibration.snapshot.json` + an offline regression test
    80	that never re-runs live — the R-DHON-4 pattern) and flip the docs to **"calibrated
    81	— directional, n=21 synthetic"** (R-DHON-3 wording; plan §4 bounds what that may
    82	ever claim). ANY floor missed → **the label DEFERS**, this doc records the numbers
    83	as they landed, and the deterministic baseline remains the only measured artifact.
    84	
    85	---
    86	
    87	## RESULTS (appended after the run — nothing above this line changes post-run)
    88	
    89	### Run #1 (2026-07-05, ~09:00–09:22 ET) — RESULTS LOST TO AN OUTPUT-PATH DEFECT (outcome-blind; no number was ever observed)
    90	
    91	The first armed pass executed all 84 live calls (~21.5 min, paced as registered)
    92	and **passed every mid-run integrity assertion** (tune phase: 21/21 real
    93	`LIVE_CLASSIFIER` verdicts, zero fallbacks — the assertion that would have
    94	aborted on degradation did not fire). It then FAILED at the final step: the
    95	harness wrote the snapshot to `lib/data/…`, a directory that no longer exists in
    96	the restructured tree (W0 moved it to `legacy/activation/lib/data/`), and
    97	`writeFileSync` threw ENOENT **after the spend and before the metrics line
    98	printed**. The per-item results existed only in memory and are unrecoverable.
    99	**No metric, floor value, or per-item outcome from run #1 was observed by anyone
   100	or any log.**
   101	
   102	**Disposition:** outcome-blind infrastructure loss — NOT a floor miss, so the
   103	pre-registered "no same-split re-run toward green" amendment is not implicated
   104	(that rule forbids outcome-driven retries; there was no outcome). Run #2 is an
   105	outcome-blind recovery re-run under the UNCHANGED protocol and floors. Harness
   106	fixes applied first (both now part of the reviewed diff): (1) the snapshot path
   107	is created + probe-written BEFORE any call is spent; (2) the snapshot is frozen
   108	BEFORE the integrity assertions so even a degraded run leaves its diagnostic
   109	record; (3) the snapshot carries an explicit `runIntegrity.degraded` flag.
   110	TPD accounting: run #1 consumed ~50K of the ~200K daily window; run #2 adds
   111	~50K — comfortably within it, re-preflighted immediately before launch.
   112	Lesson routed to `~/claude-os/tasks/lessons.md`: probe the output path before
   113	spending unrecoverable work.
   114	
   115	### Run #2 (2026-07-05, 09:24–09:45 ET) — AUTHORITATIVE. VERDICT: **the label DEFERS** (5 of 6 floors cleared; one missed)
   116	
   117	**Run integrity: CLEAN** — 21/21 tune (K=1) + 21/21 held-out (K=3) all real
   118	`LIVE_CLASSIFIER` verdicts, **zero fallbacks**, `degraded: false`, vitest exit 0.
   119	Groq `openai/gpt-oss-120b`, temp 0, `reasoningEffort:"low"`, 14s pacing, **$0**.
   120	Frozen record: `lib/data/fee-classifier-calibration.snapshot.json` (eval-locked by
   121	`evals/gold/fee-classifier-calibration.lock.test.ts` — offline, never a live re-run).
   122	
   123	**The floors (verbatim from §3.1 as pre-registered; conjunctive):**
   124	
   125	| Floor | Result | Pass |
   126	| --- | --- | --- |
   127	| Held-out accuracy ≥ 20/21, strictly > the 19/21 baseline | **20/21 = 0.952** (baseline 19/21 — strictly beaten) | ✅ |
   128	| Macro precision ≥ 0.85 | **0.971** | ✅ |
   129	| Per-class recall ≥ 0.70 (all five) | 1.00 / 1.00 / 1.00 / **0.75** / 1.00 | ✅ |
   130	| Per-class recall ≥ 0.80 on `enhanced_service_fee` + `not-a-permitted-fee` | not-a-permitted-fee **1.00** ✅ · enhanced_service_fee **0.75 (3/4)** | ❌ |
   131	| Flip-rate ≤ 0.15 (K=3) | **0.000** (63/63 unanimous) | ✅ |
   132	| Macro κ ≥ 0.60 | **0.944** | ✅ |
   133	
   134	**Verdict, per the pre-registered conjunctive rule + the owner's arming directive:
   135	the "calibrated" label DEFERS.** No re-run toward green, no floor amendment. The
   136	classifier's honest status is **"wired, env-gated, NOT calibrated"** — the
   137	deterministic 19/21 baseline remains the only floor-bearing measured artifact, and
   138	this run's numbers stand as a frozen, directional measurement (n=21, synthetic —
   139	plan §4 bounds).
   140	
   141	**The one miss, in full (nothing buried):** `relabel-test-2` — label "Service &
   142	delivery relabel fee", declared `delivery_fee`, gold `enhanced_service_fee`. All
   143	THREE reps unanimously predicted `not-a-permitted-fee`, rationale (rep-0,
   144	verbatim): "The label combines both service and delivery elements, indicating a
   145	bundled charge that cannot be described by a single permitted category." A stable,
   146	coherently-reasoned reading of the gold set's hardest case — the model treats the
   147	"&"-joined label as a bundle where the gold intends a relabeled enhanced charge.
   148	The enhanced-class recall CI95 on 3/4 is [0.30, 0.95] — a single-item miss at
   149	denominator 4, exactly why plan §4 says this gold set cannot carry a production
   150	claim in either direction.
   151	
   152	**Provenance addendum (2026-07-05, from the Codex cross-model reconciliation —
   153	appended HERE because nothing above the RESULTS marker may change post-run):**
   154	the Codex reviewer correctly observed that this file's pre-registration and
   155	results live in one uncommitted working tree, so the file alone cannot prove the
   156	rules predate the run. The precise, checkable provenance is: **(i) the six
   157	floors** — committed PRE-RUN in `docs/plan-f1b-classifier.md` §3.1 at `bda6314`
   158	(2026-07-04), including the ≥20/21 amendment at `550e3cb` (2026-07-04, the M2
   159	reconciliation) — both commits predate any live call; **(ii) the
   160	no-same-split-re-run tightening** — the owner's arming directive, committed
   161	pre-run in the HANDOFF top block at `c73c100` (2026-07-04: "NEVER re-run to
   162	green on the same split, NEVER amend a floor post-hoc"); **(iii) rep-0 as
   163	prediction-of-record and the 0/0-precision convention** — working-tree-only
   164	(this file, written before the run but with no committed boundary to prove it).
   165	Neither (iii) element was outcome-bearing on this run: all 63 scored reps were
   166	UNANIMOUS (flip-rate 0.000), so rep-0 ≡ majority ≡ any-rep — the accuracy and
   167	recall numbers are identical under every prediction-of-record convention — and
   168	every one of the five labels had ≥1 predicted positive, so the 0/0 convention
   169	was never invoked. Lesson routed to `~/claude-os/tasks/lessons.md`: COMMIT the
   170	pre-registration before arming the run, so the boundary is provable, not argued.
   171	
   172	- The live classifier **strictly beat the deterministic baseline overall** (20/21
   173	  vs 19/21) and **resolved one of the two cases the baseline structurally cannot**
   174	  (`bundle-test-2`: "Delivery & marketing combo fee" → correctly
   175	  `not-a-permitted-fee`; the keyword floor mislabels it enhanced). It did NOT

   177	  for model and baseline alike (the tune-split context echoes this: 20/21 with

   184	
   185	▸ *Plain: the AI took the locked test once, for free, with no do-overs. It scored

     1	import { readFileSync, readdirSync } from "node:fs";
     2	import { join } from "node:path";
     3	import { describe, expect, it } from "vitest";
     4	import {
     5	  CORPUS_AS_OF,
     6	  CORPUS_SEED,
     7	  LISTINGS_DRIFT_CLASSES,
     8	  applyCorpusDrift,
     9	  buildFaithfulFeed,
    10	  buildUcpResponse,
    11	  generateCatalog,
    12	  acpFeedToClaims,
    13	  ucpResponseToClaims,
    14	  runListingsVerification,
    15	} from "@/lib/packs/listings";
    16	
    17	/**
    18	 * C6 — taxonomy coverage is MEASURED, never asserted rhetorically: the eval
    19	 * computes (a) % of enumerated §7 classes with ≥1 corpus fixture injection and
    20	 * (b) % actually caught by the comparator, asserts the achieved floor, and
    21	 * prints the numbers into the test names. A guardrail scan bans "all edge
    22	 * cases"-style overclaims from the corpus and pack sources (C6/C10).
    23	 */
    24	
    25	const sor = generateCatalog(CORPUS_SEED, CORPUS_AS_OF);
    26	const faithful = buildFaithfulFeed(sor);
    27	const { feed: drifted, manifest } = applyCorpusDrift(faithful, sor);
    28	
    29	// P3-1 (W1 gate advisory): the spec-version-skew class is injected at UCP fixture
    30	// build (not by applyCorpusDrift), so DERIVE it from the committed manifest's
    31	// ucpVersionSkew block rather than hand-adding the literal — the corpus stays the
    32	// single source of truth for what was injected.
    33	const driftManifestFile = JSON.parse(
    34	  readFileSync(join(process.cwd(), "fixtures", "synthetic-restaurant", "drift-manifest.json"), "utf8"),
    35	) as { ucpVersionSkew: { class: string } };
    36	const injectedClasses = new Set<string>(manifest.map((e) => e.class));
    37	injectedClasses.add(driftManifestFile.ucpVersionSkew.class);
    38	
    39	const acpReport = runListingsVerification(acpFeedToClaims(drifted), sor);
    40	const ucpReport = runListingsVerification(
    41	  ucpResponseToClaims(
    42	    buildUcpResponse(drifted, {
    43	      supportedVersions: ["2026-03-01-draft"],
    44	      sessionId: "sess-sim-drifted-001",
    45	    }),
    46	  ),
    47	  sor,
    48	);
    49	const caughtClasses = new Set<string>(
    50	  [...acpReport.findings, ...ucpReport.findings].map((f) => f.category ?? ""),
    51	);
    52	
    53	describe("C6 measured taxonomy coverage", () => {
    54	  const total = LISTINGS_DRIFT_CLASSES.length;
    55	  const injected = LISTINGS_DRIFT_CLASSES.filter((c) => injectedClasses.has(c)).length;
    56	  const caught = LISTINGS_DRIFT_CLASSES.filter((c) => caughtClasses.has(c)).length;
    57	
    58	  it(`fixture coverage: ${injected}/${total} enumerated classes have ≥1 injection`, () => {
    59	    // The measured floor of THIS corpus (v1): every enumerated class is injected.
    60	    // This is a statement about the 8 enumerated classes, not about "all edge cases".
    61	    expect(injected).toBe(total);
    62	  });
    63	
    64	  it(`detection coverage: ${caught}/${total} injected classes are caught`, () => {
    65	    expect(caught).toBe(total);
    66	  });
    67	
    68	  it("per-class: every enumerated class with a fixture is caught somewhere", () => {
    69	    for (const c of LISTINGS_DRIFT_CLASSES) {
    70	      if (!injectedClasses.has(c)) continue;
    71	      expect(caughtClasses.has(c), `class "${c}" injected but never caught`).toBe(true);
    72	    }
    73	  });
    74	
    75	  // PER-ENTRY teeth under the class summary (M1 Codex P2 reconciliation,
    76	  // 2026-07-03): "8/8 classes caught" could in principle be satisfied by a
    77	  // false positive in the same class while a planted row goes missed. So the
    78	  // measured claim is grounded entry-by-entry: EVERY manifest entry must be
    79	  // caught as (its class, anchored to ITS row) on at least one of its labeled
    80	  // surfaces — the class tally above is the compressed summary of this.
    81	  it("per-ENTRY: every planted manifest entry is caught by class AND row on a labeled surface", () => {
    82	    for (const e of manifest) {
    83	      const reports =
    84	        e.surfaces === "both"
    85	          ? [acpReport, ucpReport]
    86	          : e.surfaces === "acp-only"
    87	            ? [acpReport]
    88	            : [ucpReport];
    89	      const caught = reports.some((rep) =>
    90	        rep.findings.some(
    91	          (f) =>
    92	            f.category === e.class &&
    93	            (f.claim.id.startsWith(`${e.targetFeedItemId}#`) ||
    94	              f.referenceRowId === e.targetFeedItemId),
    95	        ),
    96	      );
    97	      expect(caught, `${e.id} (${e.class} @ ${e.targetFeedItemId}) not caught on its surface(s)`).toBe(true);
    98	    }
    99	  });
   100	});
   101	
   102	describe("C6/C10 overclaim guardrail", () => {
   103	  const banned = /\ball (edge cases|drift|possible)/i;
   104	
   105	  it("corpus README and pack sources never claim 'all edge cases'", () => {
   106	    const files: string[] = [];
   107	    files.push(join(process.cwd(), "fixtures", "README.md"));
   108	    const fixturesDir = join(process.cwd(), "fixtures", "synthetic-restaurant");
   109	    files.push(join(fixturesDir, "README.md"));
   110	    const packDir = join(process.cwd(), "lib", "packs", "listings");
   111	    for (const f of readdirSync(packDir)) {
   112	      if (f.endsWith(".ts")) files.push(join(packDir, f));
   113	    }
   114	    for (const file of files) {
   115	      const text = readFileSync(file, "utf8");
   116	      expect(banned.test(text), `overclaim in ${file}`).toBe(false);
   117	    }
   118	  });
   119	});

     1	import { readFileSync } from "node:fs";
     2	import { describe, expect, it } from "vitest";
     3	import { accuracy, multiClassFlipRate, perClassReport, type LabeledClassification } from "@/evals/gold/metrics";
     4	import { TRUE_CATEGORY_LABELS, type TrueCategoryLabel } from "@/lib/packs/fees";
     5	
     6	/**
     7	 * EVAL-LOCK for the F1b live-classifier calibration run #2 (2026-07-05) — the
     8	 * R-DHON-4 pattern: this test reads the FROZEN snapshot
     9	 * (`lib/data/fee-classifier-calibration.snapshot.json`) and asserts (a) its
    10	 * internal consistency (every headline number recomputes from the per-item
    11	 * records through the same ported math) and (b) the run's HONEST verdict state:
    12	 * clean integrity, floors NOT all cleared, **the label DEFERS**.
    13	 *
    14	 * It makes NO live call and can never change the result — it exists so the frozen
    15	 * record cannot silently drift (an edit to the snapshot that improves any number
    16	 * without the per-item records to back it goes RED here) and so the DEFER state
    17	 * cannot quietly become a "calibrated" claim without a new, owner-gated,
    18	 * separately pre-registered run replacing this lock consciously.
    19	 */
    20	
    21	interface SnapshotItem {
    22	  id: string;
    23	  split: "tune" | "test";
    24	  trueCategory: TrueCategoryLabel;
    25	  reps: TrueCategoryLabel[];
    26	  predicted: TrueCategoryLabel;
    27	}
    28	
    29	interface Snapshot {
    30	  model: string;
    31	  K: number;
    32	  heldOut: {
    33	    n: number;
    34	    correct: number;
    35	    macroPrecision: number;
    36	    macroKappa: number;
    37	    flipRate: number;
    38	    items: SnapshotItem[];
    39	  };
    40	  floors: Record<string, { pass: boolean }>;
    41	  floorsCleared: boolean;
    42	  runIntegrity: { degraded: boolean };
    43	  misses: { id: string; predicted: string; actual: string }[];
    44	}
    45	
    46	const snapshot = JSON.parse(
    47	  readFileSync("lib/data/fee-classifier-calibration.snapshot.json", "utf8"),
    48	) as Snapshot;
    49	
    50	describe("F1b calibration eval-lock — the frozen run #2 record (R-DHON-4)", () => {
    51	  const labeled: LabeledClassification<TrueCategoryLabel>[] = snapshot.heldOut.items.map((r) => ({
    52	    id: r.id,
    53	    predicted: r.predicted,
    54	    actual: r.trueCategory,
    55	  }));
    56	
    57	  it("run integrity: authoritative (not degraded), 21 held-out items, K=3 complete, rep-0 is the prediction of record", () => {
    58	    expect(snapshot.runIntegrity.degraded).toBe(false);
    59	    expect(snapshot.heldOut.n).toBe(21);
    60	    expect(snapshot.heldOut.items).toHaveLength(21);
    61	    expect(snapshot.K).toBe(3);
    62	    for (const item of snapshot.heldOut.items) {
    63	      expect(item.reps).toHaveLength(3);
    64	      expect(item.predicted).toBe(item.reps[0]);
    65	    }
    66	  });
    67	
    68	  it("headline accuracy recomputes from the per-item records: 20/21", () => {
    69	    const correct = labeled.filter((it) => it.predicted === it.actual).length;
    70	    expect(correct).toBe(20);
    71	    expect(correct).toBe(snapshot.heldOut.correct);
    72	    expect(accuracy(labeled)).toBeCloseTo(20 / 21, 10);
    73	  });
    74	
    75	  it("the floor pattern recomputes: 5 of 6 pass; enhanced_service_fee recall 3/4 = 0.75 is the single miss", () => {
    76	    const enhanced = perClassReport(labeled, "enhanced_service_fee");
    77	    expect(enhanced.matrix).toEqual({ tp: 3, fp: 0, tn: 17, fn: 1 });
    78	    expect(enhanced.recall).toBeCloseTo(0.75, 10);
    79	    const napf = perClassReport(labeled, "not-a-permitted-fee");
    80	    expect(napf.recall).toBeCloseTo(1, 10);
    81	    // Every label's ≥0.70 floor holds; the ≥0.80 two-class floor fails ONLY on enhanced.
    82	    for (const label of TRUE_CATEGORY_LABELS) {
    83	      expect(perClassReport(labeled, label).recall, label).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(0.7);
    84	    }
    85	    expect(snapshot.floors.perClassRecallBaselineMissed.pass).toBe(false);
    86	    const passCount = Object.values(snapshot.floors).filter((f) => f.pass).length;
    87	    expect(passCount).toBe(5);
    88	  });
    89	
    90	  it("macro precision + flip-rate recompute and clear their floors", () => {
    91	    const macroP =
    92	      TRUE_CATEGORY_LABELS.reduce((sum, label) => sum + perClassReport(labeled, label).precision, 0) /
    93	      TRUE_CATEGORY_LABELS.length;
    94	    expect(macroP).toBeCloseTo(snapshot.heldOut.macroPrecision, 10);
    95	    expect(macroP).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(0.85);
    96	    const flip = multiClassFlipRate(snapshot.heldOut.items.map((r) => r.reps));
    97	    expect(flip).toBe(0);
    98	    expect(flip).toBe(snapshot.heldOut.flipRate);
    99	  });
   100	
   101	  it("THE VERDICT STATE IS LOCKED: floorsCleared=false — the label DEFERS; the single miss is relabel-test-2, unanimous", () => {
   102	    expect(snapshot.floorsCleared).toBe(false);
   103	    expect(snapshot.misses).toEqual([
   104	      { id: "relabel-test-2", predicted: "not-a-permitted-fee", actual: "enhanced_service_fee" },
   105	    ]);
   106	    const miss = snapshot.heldOut.items.find((r) => r.id === "relabel-test-2");
   107	    expect(miss?.reps).toEqual(["not-a-permitted-fee", "not-a-permitted-fee", "not-a-permitted-fee"]);
   108	  });
   109	});

docs/reviews/codex-2026-07-05-f1b-live-confirm-raw.md:836:Build update: 2026-06-28 (**A3-5 BUILT + TEST-VERIFIED (gate-2 Codex NAMED-OPEN) — the Router/Conductor agent, the 4th + final named agent; `verify` green 295+5; acceptance-gate gates 1/3/4/5 PASS, gate-2 named-open → flips SHIP 5/5 when the Codex re-confirm returns (SEAT-BLOCKED ~7:25 PM = DATED OBLIGATION batched with A3-4 round-3). UNCOMMITTED at this line; commit owner-authorized via the RESUME DIRECTIVE; PUSH HELD. NEXT = A3-6.**) — Built the Router/Conductor as the multi-critic revision-synthesis seam. `lib/agents/router.ts` = **`strongReflection`** (the STRONG deterministic multi-critic baseline + demotion fallback — reads BOTH critics, prioritizes the faithfulness failure [GATING] then surfaces the advisory domain dimensions in the same revision; a strict SUPERSET of the domain-blind `buildReflection`) + **`routerReflect`** (the LLM Router on Groq `gpt-oss-120b`, DI/mock, $0 default, **recommend-only** — `route` CLAMPED via `clampRouteToEnvelope` and never trusted; honest `FAILED_TO_FALLBACK`; the prompt withholds the raw merchant_name = no injection surface; `signals` recomputed STRUCTURALLY from inputs, never the model's word) + `criticSignals` (the structural coverage discriminator). `lib/agents/loop/orchestrator.ts` = a **`reflect?: RouterFn` seam** (default = the domain-blind `defaultReflect` — **NO loop behavior change this slice**; the strong baseline / LLM wire in as the default at A3-6) + `RevisionPlan`/`RouterFn`/`CriticSignal` types + `buildReflection` exported as the eval's RED baseline; the reflect step gets a **defensive merchant clone** (isolation, mirroring the recommend clone) and RECORDS `plan.route`/`holdForHuman` ADVISORY (recommend-only — RECORDED in the trajectory, NEVER wired to the send). **RECOMMEND-ONLY enforced two independent ways** (acceptance-gate traced both against source): the route clamp (can only ADD caution) + the post-loop isolation (the send flows ONLY through `simulate_send`; `assertEligibilityUntouched` hard-throws on any eligibility move) — **clone red-green PROVEN** (drop the reflect `{ ...merchant }` clone ⇒ the mutation-isolation test trips `R-LOOP-1b violation: … mutated … "review_required"`; restore ⇒ GREEN; raw in `docs/reviews/a3-5-verify-evidence.log`). **ANTI-THEATER EVAL (`evals/router.test.ts`, R-A3-1 — floor-not-ceiling, exactly like the Strategist A3-2 + Domain Critic A3-4):** RED `buildReflection` (domain-blind — its signature has no domain parameter) misses `domain_defective` on a multi-failure case → GREEN `strongReflection` (reads both critics) covers it as a **strict SUPERSET** (non-vacuous: the faithfulness text is byte-identical, the domain addendum is appended) → DEFER the mock Router TIES `strongReflection` on the structural axis → the **`router` label DEFERS** (the reflect step stays `"tool"`; `router` ABSENT from the trajectory, tests assert it). **THE DEFER IS STRUCTURALLY FORCED** (advisor 2026-06-28; stated verbatim in the code + eval + gate record to pre-empt the Codex "why didn't the crux agent earn?" probe): every discriminator available OFFLINE — domain coverage / which-fix-first / route — is a finite/structural axis a deterministic table reproduces BY CONSTRUCTION, so the LLM can only TIE; an LLM earns ONLY on an open-ended-quality axis scored by an INDEPENDENT CROSS-FAMILY judge — for a Groq Router that judge is Gemini ⇒ LIVE ⇒ owner-gated A3-7. So offline the Router CANNOT earn no matter how good — the anti-theater bar working as DESIGNED (AM-7), not under-delivery. **REALIZED EARNED-AGENT LEDGER (all four agents now BUILT): Drafter EARNED · Strategist DEFERRED · Domain Critic DEFERRED · Router DEFERRED = "1 earned (generation, not a finite axis) + 3 deferred (classify/decide, each tying its strong deterministic counterpart)".** **GATE:** `npm run verify` **exit 0 — 295 passed + 5 skipped** (+10 over A3-4: 8 router anti-theater + 2 agent-loop firewall/isolation), typecheck/lint/build green; differential **20/20** (`lib/core`+oracle+gold+snapshots UNTOUCHED — `git diff --name-only` confirms; 2 modified + 2 new + 2 review docs + 1 evidence log). **Codex changed-files review SEAT-BLOCKED** (usage limit, raw error surfaced ~7:25 PM, NO retry per doctrine) → **proceeding TEST-VERIFIED** (the clone red-green + the anti-theater RED-GREEN encode the load-bearing logic) with the **Codex review as a DATED OBLIGATION batched with the A3-4 round-3 re-confirm before any irreversible step** (`docs/reviews/codex-2026-06-28-a3-5-router.md`). **acceptance-gate = BLOCK (independent subagent, fresh context; gates 1/4/5 PASS — grill self-satisfied, enterprise+taste full PASS, anti-slop PASS; gate-3 PASS on the raw evidence now captured in `a3-5-verify-evidence.log`; gate-2 NAMED-OPEN — the mandatory cross-model review is seat-blocked, not waivable; NO substantive P0/P1; flips SHIP 5/5 when the batched Codex returns clean)** (`docs/reviews/gate-2026-06-28-a3-5.md`). **NEXT = A3-6** (the full multi-agent orchestrator wired — Strategist + Gemini Drafter + Domain Critic + Router all as the loop defaults — + a $0 agent-attributed trajectory fixture + `verify:full`) **after the batched Codex re-confirm**. Owner-gated stops unchanged: `git push` (HELD — no remote), deploy, public posting, spend > $5; A3-7 live Gemini run owner-gated. Everything below is historical.

Fixes: guardrail-precision coverage (the precision fix had opened a false-NEGATIVE — verb-before-noun completion claims; both the false-positive AND false-negative now closed, broad table test); partial-usage→UNKNOWN_USAGE (cost integrity); `live:true` can't bypass ENABLE_LIVE_AI (LIVE_AI_DISABLED provider boundary); budget rejects negatives/bad-cap; Gemini key via `x-goog-api-key` header; honesty copy ("claims-checked" not "truthful"; "demo makes no live calls" vs "a recorded run exists"). **NEW eval rigor (evals-specialist root-causes closed):** 45-case guardrail regression corpus ported to TS (locks the core guardrail vs the Python oracle); draft-text differential (`makeDraft` vs `out/model_runs.csv`, byte-for-byte); live-samples snapshot regression-locked; `.vercelignore` (keeps `.env`/DoorDash-string artifacts out of any upload). **Canonical live-run facts (post guardrail-fix; fixture `lib/data/live-samples.snapshot.json`): 4 LIVE_AI / 2 FAILED_TO_FALLBACK, 3 PASS / 3 WARN, 0 BLOCKED, $0.0036; all LIVE_AI drafts 3/3** (the "1 BLOCKED / 5 LIVE_AI" in the line below was the PRE-fix FIRST run — the finding that drove the precision fix; now superseded). Remaining: **owner-gated deploy only** (platform-name · Vercel · the personal-name / `.env`-hygiene / `out/`-de-brand pre-deploy checks the security audit flagged). Everything below is earlier.
docs/reviews/codex-2026-07-05-f1b-live-confirm-raw.md:1148:ActivationOps AI is currently an **offline, AI-assisted operational workflow-automation thin slice** for a DoorDash-style merchant-onboarding simulation on **dummy data**. T-001 normalizes 20 dummy merchants, scores risk deterministically, diagnoses blockers, queues high-risk merchants for human review, generates one stubbed guardrailed outreach draft, gates simulated sends behind approval, and writes audit logs — with tests T1–T18 + P2 coverage.
